At Bayer we’re visionaries, driven to solve the world’s toughest challenges and striving for a world where ,Health for all, Hunger for none’ is no longer a dream, but a real possibility. We’re doing it with energy, curiosity and sheer dedication, always learning from unique perspectives of those around us, expanding our thinking, growing our capabilities and redefining ‘impossible’. There are so many reasons to join us. If you’re hungry to build a varied and meaningful career in a community of brilliant and diverse minds to make a real difference, there’s only one choice.

Data Scientist

About The Role

Are you excited to own forecasting solutions from data extraction to production deployment? We're hiring a Senior Data Scientist for the Machine Learning & Artificial Intelligence unit within Bayer's Enterprise Data & Analytics Platform.

This is not a pure modeling role. You will extract data from source systems, wrangle messy real-world data, build statistical and ML forecast models, engineer production pipelines, evaluate results, present insights to business stakeholders, develop interactive applications, and maintain the entire infrastructure. You'll tackle unstructured problems independently and deliver measurable impact across Finance, Supply Chain, HR, Procurement, and Commercial Operations.

Our international team spans Poland, Germany, Spain, and India. We work with time series forecasting, statistical modeling, data engineering, and interactive analytics on a modern cloud-native stack. If you thrive on end-to-end ownership—from source systems to stakeholder dashboards—and enjoy solving ambiguous business problems with minimal guidance, we want to hear from you.

Key Responsibilities

 End-to-End Solution Ownership

  • Own the complete lifecycle from understanding business needs through production deployment, monitoring, and maintenance
  • Build robust ETL/ELT pipelines on Databricks; clean, validate, and transform messy real-world data at scale
  • Transform loosely defined business questions into structured solutions independently—identifying data gaps, proposing approaches, and iterating based on feedback

Forecasting & Modeling

  • Design and deploy production-grade forecasting solutions using statistical models (ARIMA, ETS, BSTS) and ML approaches (XGBoost, LightGBM, neural networks)
  • Engineer sophisticated features: lag features, rolling statistics, external signals, calendar effects, and domain-specific transformations
  • Implement forecast reconciliation and hierarchical aggregation for complex business structures
  • Establish rigorous evaluation frameworks: backtesting, time series cross-validation, accuracy metrics, prediction intervals, and drift monitoring

Software Engineering & Infrastructure

  • Write production-grade Python and R code with modular architecture, comprehensive testing, error handling, and documentation
  • Build and maintain sophisticated R Shiny applications with integrated JavaScript components
  • Orchestrate ML pipelines using Kubeflow for automated training, validation, deployment, experiment tracking, and model versioning
  • Manage infrastructure as code: Databricks workspaces, Azure resources, CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps), containerization, and secrets management

Analysis, Debugging & Monitoring

  • Troubleshoot complex issues across the full stack: data pipeline failures, model degradation, API errors, and integration problems
  • Implement continuous monitoring: automated data quality checks, feature drift detection, performance tracking, and alerting systems
  • Conduct root cause analysis of forecast errors, identify data anomalies, validate business logic, and communicate findings clearly

Required Qualifications

 Technical Foundation

  • Education & Experience: Master's or PhD with 3+ years delivering end-to-end data science solutions in production
  • Programming: Strong Python, R and SQL proficiency
  • Forecasting Expertise: Time series decomposition, seasonality, trend analysis, ensemble methods, probabilistic forecasting, hierarchical reconciliation
  • Data Engineering: Databricks/Spark/PySpark, Delta Lake, ETL/ELT design, job orchestration, performance tuning
  • KNIME: Building analytical workflows, data preprocessing, model pipelines, and system integration

End-to-End Capabilities

  • MLOps: Kubeflow pipeline orchestration, experiment tracking, model registry, automated deployment
  • Software Engineering: Git workflows, code reviews, testing frameworks (pytest, testthat), modular design, documentation
  • Application Development: Build RESTful APIs and R Shiny applications from scratch; handle authentication, deployment, and optimization
  • Cloud Infrastructure: Azure services (Databricks, Blob Storage, Data Factory, Key Vault, Functions), container orchestration, CI/CD

Essential Soft Skills

  • Autonomy: Self-starter who can take vague requirements and independently drive projects from concept to production
  • Problem-Solving: Systematic debugging across the full stack—from source data to infrastructure
  • Business Acumen: Translate business needs into technical solutions; understand when "good enough" beats "perfect"
  • Communication: Present technical work clearly to non-technical audiences; influence decisions with data; write comprehensive documentation
  • Collaboration: Work effectively with cross-functional teams while maintaining end-to-end ownership

Preferred (Not Required)

 SAP HANA/BW experience, advanced Kubeflow capabilities, Terraform, Kubernetes, PowerBI/Tableau integration, data governance frameworks, multilingual capability

Language Requirement: Fluent in English (written and spoken); additional languages from our team regions are a plus.

YOUR APPLICATION

This is your opportunity to tackle the world’s biggest challenges with us: Maintaining our health, feeding growing populations and slowing the rate of climate change. You have a voice, ideas and perspectives and we want to hear them. Because our success begins with you. Be part of something big. Be Bayer.

Bayer welcomes applications from all individuals, regardless of race, national origin, gender, age, physical characteristics, social origin, disability, union membership, religion, family status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression or any unlawful criterion under applicable law. We are committed to treating all applicants fairly and avoiding discrimination.

Title: Data Scientist - Analytics and ML
Description:
Overview

WELCOME TO SITA

At SITA, we keep airports moving, airlines flying smoothly, and borders open. Our technology and communication innovations power the success of the global air travel industry.

You’ll find us in 95% of international airports, working closely with over 2,500 transportation and government clients. Each partnership brings unique challenges, and we thrive on delivering fresh solutions and cutting-edge tech to keep operations running like clockwork. We don’t just move the world forward—we’re proud to be recognized as a Great Place to Work® by 79% of our employees and certified in most of our growing locations. Here, we feel empowered, supported, and inspired to grow.

Are you ready to love your job?

The adventure begins right here, with you, at SITA.

About The Role & Team

As Data Scientist – Machine Learning and Analytics, you will make the operational data of OCC Assistant Manager — SITA's AI-powered Operations Control Center solution for airline disruption management — speak: automatically reconstructing disruptions from Ops Control and Crew Control data, explaining how airlines resolved them and quantifying the value created by OCC Assistant Manager.

You will analyze operational database changes such as delays, aircraft swaps, crew swaps and operational activities, build trusted analytical models, measure OCC Assistant Manager usage and solution quality, and create business cases that show airline savings.

Reporting to the Head of Disruption Management Solutions (OCC Assistant Manager), you will be part of the OCC Assistant Manager team.

What You'll Do


Design and build the databases, data models and processes needed to analyze operational data
Reconstruct operational disruptions automatically from Ops Control and Crew Control database changes
Aggregate events such as aircraft swaps, crew swaps, delays and operational activities into a clear view of what happened during the day
Analyze how the airline resolved each disruption, whether OCC Assistant Manager was used and whether OCC Assistant Manager proposed good solutions
Create customer-facing reports, dashboards and business cases that quantify OCC Assistant Manager value, savings and operational impact
Create internal KPIs, metrics and management reporting to improve the product and understand customer operations
Apply machine learning where it adds value: disruption detection, pattern detection, forecasting, anomaly detection or risk scoring
Support Science, onboarding and automatic OCC Assistant Manager configuration with exploratory analysis and data-driven insight


Qualifications

EXPERIENCE


Demonstrated ability to write advanced SQL queries and analyze complex operational database changes over time
Proficiency in Python for data processing, event aggregation and analysis, using pandas, scikit-learn or similar
Ability to define KPIs, value metrics and business cases that connect operational events to customer savings
Proven capability to identify patterns in operational changes and determine when to use machine learning versus simpler analysis
Experience building clear dashboards and analytical products that explain disruptions, resolutions and OCC Assistant Manager value
Ability to validate whether data is correct from an operational and business perspective
Strong communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with technical, operational and customer-facing stakeholders
A curious, rigorous mindset, comfortable with messy real-world operational data
Motivation to learn airline OCC workflows, crew-control concepts and the OCC Assistant Manager data model


NICE-TO-HAVE


Experience with airline operations, Ops Control, Crew Control, OCC KPIs or disruption management


What We Offer

We're all about diversity. We operate in 200 countries and speak 60 different languages and cultures. We're really proud of our inclusive environment. Our offices are comfortable and fun places to work, and we make sure you get to work from home too. Find out what it's like to join our team and take a step closer to your best life ever.

🏡 Flex Week: Work from home up to 2 days/week (depending on your team's needs)

⏰ Flex Day: Make your workday suit your life and plans.

🌎 Flex-Location: Take up to 30 days a year to work from any location in the world.

🌿 Employee Wellbeing: We have got you covered with our Employee Assistance Program (EAP), for you and your dependents 24/7, 365 days/year. We also offer Champion Health - a personalized platform that supports a range of wellbeing needs.

🚀 Professional Development: Level up your skills with our training platforms, including LinkedIn Learning!

🙌 Competitive Benefits: Competitive benefits that make sense with both your local market and employment status.

SITA is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value a diverse workforce. In support of our Employment Equity Program, we encourage women, aboriginal people, members of visible minorities, and/or persons with disabilities to apply and self-identify in the application process.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist




FLiiTS es una de las mayores flotas de food delivery de España. En solo un año y medio, hemos desarrollado la tecnología y los procesos necesarios para escalar desde cero hasta gestionar miles de repartidores en España, Polonia y pronto otros países de Europa.




Trabajamos en un entorno muy dinámico, con problemas complejos de optimización, operaciones y datos que requieren agilidad, capacidad analítica y una fuerte orientación a la ejecución.




En FLiiTS buscamos a una persona con gran capacidad analítica, gusto por programar y muchas ganas de aprender. Trabajarás mano a mano con el VP de Data en el desarrollo de algunos de los sistemas más críticos de la compañía.




Es un rol muy orientado a la ejecución. Esperamos que puedas entender los problemas de negocio, diseñar soluciones y llevarlas a producción con rapidez. También es una de las posiciones con mayor recorrido dentro de FLiiTS: buscamos a alguien con potencial para crecer junto al área de Data y asumir progresivamente más responsabilidad.




Qué harás




Desarrollar agentes de IA para automatizar procesos internos y mejorar la productividad de los equipos.
Diseñar e implementar modelos de optimización (MILP) para la planificación de turnos de miles de repartidores.
Desarrollar algoritmos de dispatching, routing y asignación de pedidos a repartidores en tiempo real mediante diferentes técnicas de optimización (matching, blossom y MILP).
Diseñar y mantener los sistemas de cálculo de nóminas y pagos, incentivos de los repartidores, scoring y performance.
Construir pipelines y procesos de datos con Python, SQL y Dagster.
Contruir modelos predictivos de ML para diferentes casos de uso, como por ejemplo la predicción de absentismo.
Resolver cualquier otro problema que aparezca en FLiiTS y tenga como mecanismo de resolución el manejo de grandes volúmenes de datos.
Llevar tus desarrollos a producción colaborando estrechamente con el equipo de backend, programando en Python y Kotlin.




No esperamos que domines todas estas áreas desde el primer día. Buscamos a alguien con una base técnica sólida y capacidad para aprender rápidamente a medida que se enfrenta a nuevos retos.




Qué buscamos




No buscamos necesariamente a la persona con más años de experiencia, sino a alguien con el potencial, la perseverancia y la ambición necesarios para crecer muy rápido.




Valoramos especialmente:




Formación en Ingeniería, Matemáticas, Física o disciplinas cuantitativas similares (Ingeniería Industrial, Caminos, Informática, Matemáticas, Física, etc.).
Muy buen nivel de Python y SQL.
Gran capacidad analítica y facilidad para aprender tecnologías nuevas.
Gusto por programar y construir producto.
Perseverancia para resolver problemas complejos.
Mentalidad de ownership: identificar problemas, proponer soluciones y llevarlas hasta producción.
Autonomía, curiosidad y ganas de asumir responsabilidad desde el primer día.




Se valorarán ganas de aprender en optimización matemática, algoritmos, LLMs, Kotlin, Docker, AWS y orquestación de datos con Dagster.




Qué ofrecemos




Modelo de trabajo híbrido, con oficina en Barcelona (Sants). 3 días presencial y 2 remoto. Máxima flexibilidad en fechas especiales como verano o navidad.
La oportunidad de trabajar en problemas reales de optimización, inteligencia artificial y operaciones con impacto directo en miles de repartidores.
Mucha autonomía para llevar proyectos completos desde la identificación del problema hasta su puesta en producción.
Aprendizaje directo junto a un equipo con mucha experiencia y colaboración estrecha con los equipos de ingeniería y operaciones.
Un entorno joven, dinámico y de rápido crecimiento, con poca jerarquía y contacto directo entre equipos.
Posibilidades reales de asumir progresivamente más responsabilidad dentro del área de Data.
Retribución flexible a través de Coverflex.
Café ilimitado en la oficina.
Actividades de equipo y eventos internos.




Cómo aplicar




Envíanos tu CV a people.rrhh@fliits.com o inscríbete a través de LinkedIn.

Title: Científico de datos
Description:
Global Exchange Group es una multinacional española, especializada en servicios de cambio de moneda para los segmentos B2B2C. Somos uno de los líderes mundiales del sector, contando con una red de más de 500 oficinas en más de 90 aeropuertos internacionales de 29 países de los 5 continentes.




Global Exchange Tecnologías Digitales (GETD) es la división tecnológica digital del Grupo Global Exchange, y en la actualidad integramos el área de tecnología y de negocio digital para ofrecer las soluciones más innovadoras al negocio. Nuestro desarrollo profesional se centra en la formación continua, en la incorporación de metodologías ágiles y de las últimas tecnologías, así como en la contratación de perfiles diversos y versátiles para adaptarnos a la realidad cambiante del sector.




Actualmente estamos buscando un Data Scientist para nuestro equipo de Soluciones y Datos, pieza fundamental dentro del área tecnológica de Global Exchange.




¿Qué estamos buscando?




Formación:

Ingeniería Informática
Estadística
Equivalentes con sólida base analítica




Experiencia: 2 años trabajando en el ciclo completo del dato




Requisitos técnicos:

Imprescindible: SQL avanzado y Python

Construcción y despliegue de modelos de machine learning en producción:

Series temporales (forecasting, detección de anomalías)
Clasificación (supervisada y no supervisada)
Sistemas de recomendación

Experiencia en despliegues batch e inferencia en tiempo real

Experiencia en ingesta, transformación y distribución de datos en entornos productivos

Manejo de herramientas BI para análisis y visualización (preferiblemente Qlik o Power BI)




¿Qué ofrecemos?

Contrato indefinido
Proyecto innovador y apasionante
Trabajar en una multinacional presente en todos los continentes




¿Te lo vas a perder?




En cumplimiento de lo dispuesto en la normativa vigente en materia de protección de datos, le informamos que los datos personales que facilite en el marco del presente proceso de selección serán tratados por Eurodivisas Corporación, S.L., en calidad de responsable del tratamiento, con la finalidad de gestionar su candidatura, evaluar su perfil profesional y, en su caso, incluirle en futuros procesos de selección. La base jurídica para este tratamiento es el consentimiento del interesado y/o la aplicación de medidas precontractuales. Los datos no serán cedidos a terceros, salvo obligación legal, y se conservarán durante el tiempo estrictamente necesario para cumplir con la finalidad mencionada. Puede ejercer sus derechos de acceso, rectificación, supresión, oposición, limitación del tratamiento, portabilidad, así como retirar su consentimiento en cualquier momento, dirigiéndose por escrito a la siguiente dirección de correo electrónico: lopd@eurodivisas.com, adjuntando copia de su documento de identidad e identificando el derecho o conjunto de derechos que desea ejercer. Para más información sobre el tratamiento de sus datos personales, consulte nuestra política de privacidad a través del siguiente enlace: https://www.global-exchange.com/politica-de-privacidad

Title: AI Applications & Data Science Consultant
Description:
Company Description

Talan – Positive Innovation

Talan is an international consulting group specializing in innovation and business transformation through technology. With over 7,200 consultants in 21 countries and a turnover of €850M, we are committed to delivering impactful, future-ready solutions.

Talan at a Glance

Headquartered in Paris and operating globally, Talan combines technology, innovation, and empowerment to deliver measurable results for our clients. Over the past 22 years, we’ve built a strong presence in the IT and consulting landscape, and we’re on track to reach €1 billion in revenue this year.

Our Core Areas of Expertise


Data & Technologies: We design and implement large-scale, end-to-end architecture and data solutions, including data integration, data science, visualization, Big Data, AI, and Generative AI.
Cloud & Application Services: We integrate leading platforms such as SAP, Salesforce, Oracle, Microsoft, AWS, and IBM Maximo, helping clients transition to the cloud and improve operational efficiency.
Management & Innovation Consulting: We lead business and digital transformation initiatives through project and change management best practices (PM, PMO, Agile, Scrum, Product Ownership), and support domains such as Supply Chain, Cybersecurity, and ESG/Low-Carbon strategies.


We work with major global clients across diverse sectors, including Transport & Logistics, Financial Services, Energy & Utilities, Retail, and Media & Telecommunications.

Job Description

You will join a leading global R&D environment where advanced AI-driven applications support researchers in their daily work—from data analysis and statistical modelling to predictive insights and dashboarding.

These applications, built mainly with Shiny (R) and Streamlit (Python), are part of a growing analytics ecosystem running on a dedicated internal platform. The mission combines maintenance of existing AI/statistical tools and development of new data-driven applications based on real user needs.

You will act as a bridge between business users (researchers) and technical teams, ensuring applications remain reliable, scalable, and relevant.

What You Will Do


Investigate and fix issues in AI algorithms (bugs, performance, data pipeline failures)
Maintain and improve existing Shiny (R) and Streamlit (Python) applications
Ensure stability after platform or dependency updates
Develop new interactive modules (dashboards, analytics tools, reporting apps)
Analyse business needs and translate them into technical solutions
Optimize and refactor Python and R codebases
Manage data pipelines and connectors (APIs, databases, files)
Conduct testing (UAT, unit tests, integration tests)
Collaborate with data scientists, statisticians, and business users
Document all developments and processes (French & English)


Qualifications

What We Are Looking For

Required:


3–5 years of experience in Python and/or R development
Experience with Shiny (R) and/or Streamlit (Python)
Strong debugging and problem-solving skills
Experience maintaining production-level applications
Solid knowledge of:
Data manipulation (Pandas / dplyr)
Data visualisation (ggplot2, plotly, matplotlib, altair)
Experience with Git/GitHub (PRs, branching, versioning)
Understanding of basic AI/ML concepts (evaluation metrics, overfitting, pipelines)
Ability to work in a bilingual environment (French & English)


Nice to have:


Experience with GCP (BigQuery, Vertex AI)
Knowledge of Docker and APIs (FastAPI, REST)
Exposure to ML frameworks (scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch)
Statistical knowledge (PCA, hypothesis testing, DOE)
Experience with Posit / Shiny Server environments
Familiarity with NLP or computer vision concepts


Additional Information

What do we offer you?

We offer:


Full-time and permanent employment contract
Flexible work schedule
Professional growth possibilities & learning opportunities
Possibility to join a multicultural team & work in the international environment
Variety of benefits to support your physical, emotional and financial wellbeing: private medical insurance, life insurance, lunch and transport card as part of the flexible remuneration pack, and online language classes
Smart Office Pack


If you are passionate about data, development & tech, we want to meet you!

Title: Data Scientist – MarTech (Measurement & Optimization)
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

The MarTech Data Science team drives performance marketing efficiency through advanced measurement, optimization, and scalable modeling.

We operate in a highly complex and ambiguous environment where:


Ground truth is often unobservable (incrementality vs attribution)
Clean experimentation is not always feasible
Decisions must be made under uncertainty with imperfect data


We build solutions for budget allocation, incrementality measurement, and bidding, enabling the business to invest each euro where it generates the highest impact.

YOUR MISSION

We are looking for a Data Scientist to own models and features end-to-end within larger MarTech initiatives, and contribute to the team's measurement and optimization solutions. Operating at a global level, you will design solutions that serve the entire Delivery Hero portfolio, including brands like Glovo, Talabat, and PedidosYa. You will work autonomously on well-defined problems and collaborate closely with senior team members on more ambiguous ones.

This role requires solid foundations in causal inference and modeling, combined with the ability to communicate clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders and translate marketing problems into reliable, production-grade solutions.

THE JOURNEY

Own features and components end-to-end within MarTech measurement & optimization


Take ownership of models and features inside larger initiatives, from data exploration to production deployment
Contribute to defining success metrics and modeling approaches, with senior guidance on more ambiguous problem framings
Balance methodological rigor with business constraints and timelines


Contribute to decision frameworks under ambiguity


Help translate marketing questions into structured, model-driven analyses
Operate in environments where experimentation is limited or infeasible, applying established methods across MMM, experiments, and observational analysis
Make and document assumptions explicitly, and flag their impact on decisions


Support decision-making under uncertainty


Provide clear analyses despite imperfect measurement, articulating trade-offs and limitations
Identify conflicting signals (e.g., attribution vs incrementality vs MMM) and discuss them with senior team members
Ensure outputs are actionable and aligned with real business constraints (budget caps, pacing, channel dependencies)


Apply methodological rigor and contribute to validation


Contribute to and apply frameworks across MMM, incrementality testing (geo experiments, synthetic control), bidding, and/or LTV
Apply validation strategies in the absence of ground truth (cross-method validation, backtesting, sensitivity analysis)
Follow team standards for statistical rigor, interpretability, and reproducibility
Participate in knowledge sharing within the chapter and team


Communicate effectively with stakeholders


Translate modeling outputs into clear narratives for non-technical stakeholders
Communicate with squad-level stakeholders, adapting abstraction level appropriately
Address questions on model outputs with appropriate context and honesty about limitations


Build production-grade systems


Develop reliable, maintainable solutions with good standards in testing, monitoring, documentation, and reproducibility
Work closely with Engineering and Product to deploy and improve systems
Ensure long-term usability of models as decision products, not just analyses


Qualifications

Statistical, Causal & Predictive Foundations

Solid foundation in experimentation, causal inference, time-series, and predictive modeling. Experience applying these methods in real-world settings with noisy or biased data.

Working Effectively Under Ambiguity


Make sound progress on partially-defined problems, escalating when blocked
Provide reasoned analyses under uncertainty
Recognize trade-offs between conflicting methodologies and signals


Feature & Model Ownership

Track record of owning models and features from problem definition → modeling approach → production → adoption, with senior input on more open-ended initiatives.

MarTech Domain Exposure

Working understanding of attribution, digital and offline channels, performance marketing, bidding, and budget allocation. Familiarity with incrementality, diminishing returns, and cross-channel interactions.

Stakeholder Communication


Communicate statistical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Present model results clearly and discuss limitations honestly
Build trust through reliable, well-documented work


Engineering & Production Skills

Strong Python and SQL skills (e.g., BigQuery). Experience with modular design, testing, version control, and modern data/ML stacks. Exposure to agentic workflows (LLM-driven or tool-using agents) is a plus.

Pragmatic Approach

Familiarity with methods such as uplift modeling, MMM, quasi-experiments, and survival models. Ability to apply established methods to deliver real business impact.

Additional Information

We believe driven talent deserves:


🍔 Monthly Glovo credits to satisfy your cravings!
🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.
🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.


Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds – all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Delve into our culture by taking a peek at our Instagram and check out our Linkedin and website!

At Glovo, your success is defined by both results and behaviors. We hire for excellence in craft and for the Leadership Principles that shape how we think, decide, and collaborate. What you achieve matters and how you achieve it defines us. Together, they move the business forward and deliver great experiences. Learn more about our Leadership Principles here.

Title: Growth Data Scientist
Description:
Smadex is a leading advertising technology company founded in Barcelona in 2010 and sold to American-based and stock-listed Entravision in 2018 (NYSE::EVC). We are one of the top mobile ad-tech companies in the world and the largest Demand Side Platform (DSP) based in Europe, with our revenues over +$100M and consistently growing +40% YoY over the past years. And with an incredible 100% growth last year.




Our mission is to continue to improve our ad-tech platform, especially the core of it, our algorithms, to help our clients achieve their programmatic advertising campaign goals. We want to give our employees a job they’ll love, where they will be challenged to improve results through real-life engineering and data analysis and where everyone’s implication has an impact.




We are looking for a Growth Data Scientist to work closely with internal and external teams to help advertisers achieve their business goals and improve the effectiveness of Smadex Ads. The candidate should be communicative and analytical, in order to answer the complex questions that Product and Campaign performance are facing. For this purpose we use tons of data available to get powerful insights to resolve them, creating effective strategies to help improve the Smadex platform!




This is a full-time position based in Barcelona.




Your tasks and responsibilities:

Dive deep into complex datasets, covering the behavior of millions of users and apps worldwide, to extract actionable learnings that inform high-level strategy and stakeholder understanding
Partner with business and technical teams to drive rigorous analysis and testing, identifying key opportunities to maximize the performance and effectiveness of Smadex campaigns.
Collaborate with stakeholders to channel market insights and performance data into our roadmap, directly influencing Product and ML strategies to enhance our platform's capabilities.




Skills and requirements:

+3 years of experience as a Data Analyst / Data Scientist
Bachelor's or Master's Degree in a Quantitative Field (Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Sciences,.)
Strong problem-solving skills, ability to formulate questions, run analysis to articulate a recommendation.
Proven working knowledge of Python, SQL and large data sets, understanding of analytic methodologies for data evaluation.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present analysis and conclusions.
Strong knowledge and experience of statistical concepts and hypothesis testing.
Knowledge of the ad-tech industry is a plus.




Please note that we do NOT provide VISA sponsorship. Candidates without a legal permit to work in Spain won't be considered.




What is in it for you:




Integrate a highly motivated and young team.
Great compensation package.
Top location at the heart of Barcelona in a penthouse office with a rooftop terrace, barbeque, and a fully stocked fridge.
Work from home: 2 days/week
Private Health Insurance
Access to LinkedIn Learning
Meal vouchers - Tickets Restaurant monthly allowance.
Monthly gym allowance: Choose between DiR Gym and Wellhub
Monthly TGIF events.
Regular team-building events.
Fun and friendly work environment with talented marketers and engineers from over 40+ countries
And more!

Title: Mid Data Scientist
Description:
Desde InnoIT estamos buscando un/a Optimization Scientist para incorporarse a un proyecto internacional enfocado en la resolución de problemas operativos complejos.

Sobre el proyecto

Formarás parte de un equipo especializado en el desarrollo de soluciones avanzadas de optimización. Tu misión será transformar necesidades y reglas de negocio en modelos matemáticos y algoritmos capaces de mejorar la toma de decisiones y la eficiencia operativa.




¿Qué harás?

Diseñar e implementar algoritmos clásicos y metaheurísticos.
Trabajar con técnicas como Genetic Algorithms, Particle Swarm Optimization y Simulated Annealing.
Traducir reglas de negocio complejas en restricciones y modelos matemáticos.
Desarrollar soluciones de optimización adaptadas a diferentes desafíos operativos.
Recopilar, limpiar y preparar datos procedentes de distintas fuentes.
Evaluar y ajustar el rendimiento de los algoritmos.
Realizar análisis de sensibilidad sobre parámetros y restricciones.
Colaborar con equipos de producto para llevar los modelos a producción.
Comunicar resultados y conclusiones a stakeholders y responsables senior.




Requisitos

Formación en Investigación Operativa, Matemáticas Aplicadas, Informática o áreas relacionadas.
Entre 3 y 4 años de experiencia en optimización o posiciones similares.
Experiencia sólida con algoritmos metaheurísticos.
Dominio de Python.
Experiencia con Pandas, NumPy, SciPy y Scikit-learn.
Conocimiento de herramientas como PuLP, Pyomo, DEAP, Mealpy u Optuna.
Experiencia en modelado de reglas de negocio y programación por restricciones.
Inglés fluido.




Valorable

Experiencia con Gurobi, Hexaly, CPLEX u OR-Tools.
Conocimientos de optimización lineal.
Experiencia con procesos ETL y limpieza de datos.
Conocimientos de visualización de datos.
Experiencia desplegando modelos en AWS.
Experiencia en modelado matemático avanzado.




¿Qué ofrecemos?

⏱️ Horario flexible.

🏠 Modelo de trabajo híbrido, favoreciendo el equilibrio entre vida personal y profesional.

💰 Salario competitivo y plan de retribución flexible.

🩺 Seguro médico privado.

🎓 Presupuesto anual para formación y desarrollo profesional.

📚 Cursos de idiomas.

🤓 Participación en comunidades tecnológicas y eventos del sector.

🔄 Seguimiento continuo y acompañamiento profesional.

🚀 Plan de desarrollo y crecimiento adaptado a tus objetivos.

En InnoIT creemos que el talento se desarrolla en un entorno donde aprender, innovar y disfrutar del camino van de la mano.




🚀 Únete a nuestro equipo, participa en proyectos innovadores y sigue creciendo junto a profesionales apasionados por la tecnología.




Si quieres saber más sobre nosotros:

👉 https://www.inno-it.es/

👉 InnoIT Consulting Barcelona Meetup:

https://www.meetup.com/es-ES/Meetup-de-InnoIT-Consulting-en-Barcelona/

👉 InnoIT Consulting Madrid Meetup:

https://www.meetup.com/es-ES/meetup-de-innoit-consulting-en-madrid/

Diversidad, Equidad e Inclusión

En InnoIT promovemos activamente la diversidad, la equidad y la inclusión. Damos la bienvenida a personas con todo tipo de perfiles, experiencias y trayectorias profesionales, y fomentamos especialmente la participación de colectivos tradicionalmente infrarrepresentados en el sector tecnológico.

Evaluamos todas las candidaturas de manera objetiva y justa, garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades durante todo el proceso de selección. Asimismo, nos comprometemos a realizar los ajustes razonables necesarios para asegurar una experiencia accesible e inclusiva.

No discriminamos por razón de género, edad, origen, nacionalidad, orientación sexual, identidad o expresión de género, religión, discapacidad o cualquier otra condición protegida por la legislación vigente.

Trabajamos para construir un entorno laboral diverso, respetuoso e inclusivo, donde todas las personas tengan las mismas oportunidades de crecimiento, desarrollo y reconocimiento profesional.

Si te interesa trabajar en un entorno internacional con tecnologías modernas, ¡queremos conocerte!

Desde InnoIT estamos buscando un/a Optimization Scientist para incorporarse a un proyecto internacional enfocado en la resolución de problemas operativos complejos.

Sobre el proyecto

Formarás parte de un equipo especializado en el desarrollo de soluciones avanzadas de optimización. Tu misión será transformar necesidades y reglas de negocio en modelos matemáticos y algoritmos capaces de mejorar la toma de decisiones y la eficiencia operativa.


¿Qué harás?

  • Diseñar e implementar algoritmos clásicos y metaheurísticos.
  • Trabajar con técnicas como Genetic Algorithms, Particle Swarm Optimization y Simulated Annealing.
  • Traducir reglas de negocio complejas en restricciones y modelos matemáticos.
  • Desarrollar soluciones de optimización adaptadas a diferentes desafíos operativos.
  • Recopilar, limpiar y preparar datos procedentes de distintas fuentes.
  • Evaluar y ajustar el rendimiento de los algoritmos.
  • Realizar análisis de sensibilidad sobre parámetros y restricciones.
  • Colaborar con equipos de producto para llevar los modelos a producción.
  • Comunicar resultados y conclusiones a stakeholders y responsables senior.


Requisitos

  • Formación en Investigación Operativa, Matemáticas Aplicadas, Informática o áreas relacionadas.
  • Entre 3 y 4 años de experiencia en optimización o posiciones similares.
  • Experiencia sólida con algoritmos metaheurísticos.
  • Dominio de Python.
  • Experiencia con Pandas, NumPy, SciPy y Scikit-learn.
  • Conocimiento de herramientas como PuLP, Pyomo, DEAP, Mealpy u Optuna.
  • Experiencia en modelado de reglas de negocio y programación por restricciones.
  • Inglés fluido.


Valorable

  • Experiencia con Gurobi, Hexaly, CPLEX u OR-Tools.
  • Conocimientos de optimización lineal.
  • Experiencia con procesos ETL y limpieza de datos.
  • Conocimientos de visualización de datos.
  • Experiencia desplegando modelos en AWS.
  • Experiencia en modelado matemático avanzado.


¿Qué ofrecemos?

⏱️ Horario flexible.

🏠 Modelo de trabajo híbrido, favoreciendo el equilibrio entre vida personal y profesional.

💰 Salario competitivo y plan de retribución flexible.

🩺 Seguro médico privado.

🎓 Presupuesto anual para formación y desarrollo profesional.

📚 Cursos de idiomas.

🤓 Participación en comunidades tecnológicas y eventos del sector.

🔄 Seguimiento continuo y acompañamiento profesional.

🚀 Plan de desarrollo y crecimiento adaptado a tus objetivos.

En InnoIT creemos que el talento se desarrolla en un entorno donde aprender, innovar y disfrutar del camino van de la mano.


🚀 Únete a nuestro equipo, participa en proyectos innovadores y sigue creciendo junto a profesionales apasionados por la tecnología.


Si quieres saber más sobre nosotros:

👉 https://www.inno-it.es/

👉 InnoIT Consulting Barcelona Meetup:

https://www.meetup.com/es-ES/Meetup-de-InnoIT-Consulting-en-Barcelona/

👉 InnoIT Consulting Madrid Meetup:

https://www.meetup.com/es-ES/meetup-de-innoit-consulting-en-madrid/

Diversidad, Equidad e Inclusión

En InnoIT promovemos activamente la diversidad, la equidad y la inclusión. Damos la bienvenida a personas con todo tipo de perfiles, experiencias y trayectorias profesionales, y fomentamos especialmente la participación de colectivos tradicionalmente infrarrepresentados en el sector tecnológico.

Evaluamos todas las candidaturas de manera objetiva y justa, garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades durante todo el proceso de selección. Asimismo, nos comprometemos a realizar los ajustes razonables necesarios para asegurar una experiencia accesible e inclusiva.

No discriminamos por razón de género, edad, origen, nacionalidad, orientación sexual, identidad o expresión de género, religión, discapacidad o cualquier otra condición protegida por la legislación vigente.

Trabajamos para construir un entorno laboral diverso, respetuoso e inclusivo, donde todas las personas tengan las mismas oportunidades de crecimiento, desarrollo y reconocimiento profesional.

Si te interesa trabajar en un entorno internacional con tecnologías modernas, ¡queremos conocerte!

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About Us

Prime Insights is a Sweden-based technology company reaching millions of diverse users through our proprietary Market Research Panel. Beyond our world-class Market Research Panel, we are scaling our Offerwall and Play-to-Earn (P2E) ecosystems. We connect millions of users to games and products they love, leveraging sophisticated targeting to ensure high engagement for advertisers and maximum rewards for our users.

As part of our Central Data Team, you will be the backbone of our intelligence, building the models and systems that power our international community.




We are looking for a Data Scientist who can build predictive models, recommendation systems, and experimentation frameworks to improve user engagement, LTV, and fraud detection in a high-scale product environment. Experience in AdTech, Gaming, or similar industries is a strong plus.




What You’ll Do

Predictive Targeting & Personalization: Build recommendation engines that match the right offer (game/survey) to the right user at the right time to maximise user engagement.
User Lifetime Value (LTV) Modeling: Develop models to predict long-term user value and churn, allowing us to optimize user acquisition spend.
Fraud & Anomaly Detection: Guard the ecosystem by building real-time systems to detect "farming," bot behavior, and VPN spoofing.
Statistical Rigor: Serve as the internal consultant for experimentation (A/B testing) design, ensuring results are statistically significant and unbiased.
Advanced Analytics: Move beyond descriptive reporting into diagnostic and predictive analytics.




What We’re Looking For

3+ years of experience in Data Science, ideally within AdTech, Gaming, or FinTech.
Core Tech Stack: Expert proficiency in Python (Scikit-learn, XGBoost/LightGBM/CatBoost, PyTorch) and Advanced SQL.
AdTech Knowledge: Familiarity with concepts like CPM, ROAS, Attribution, and Post-back loops.
Strong communication skills: Able to clearly explain findings, assumptions, and tradeoffs to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Experimental Design: Deep understanding of A/B testing in high-traffic environments where "noise" is high.
Product Curiosity: You don’t just build models; you want to understand the psychology of why a user clicks one game over another.




How to Apply

Use the Easy Apply button.

❗Important: We are not considering freelancers working on multiple projects simultaneously. This is a full-time position for a dedicated Data Scientist who is ready to commit to a single product ecosystem and contribute meaningfully to its long-term success.

Title: Expert Data Scientist (m/f/d)
Description:
Company Description

T‑Systems is part of the Deutsche Telekom Group, with around 30.000 employees worldwide. We create technology with purpose to generate a positive impact on society. We are looking for curious talent, eager to learn, take on challenges, and contribute ideas that transform our customers’ experience.

We trust people: we offer autonomy, continuous support, and a collaborative environment where you can grow without limits. We are one global team, guided by respect, integrity, and a passion for doing better every day.

Job Description

Project Description:

We develop scalable data solutions for internal customers with a strong focus on self-service capabilities and automation. Our goal is to enable customers to create and extend their own use cases through standardized data onboarding processes and reusable solution building blocks.

Activity Description And Concrete Tasks


Design and implement scalable data solutions for internal customer use cases
Partner with stakeholders to understand business challenges, translate them into technical solutions, and pressent results
Develop reusable and scalable solution architectures instead of isolated proof-of-concept implementations
Build, optimize, and maintain ETL workflows, data pipelines, and onboarding processes for new data sources
Drive automation and continuously improve self-service capabilities for internal customers
Develop and maintain GitLab-based CI/CD pipelines for automated testing, deployment, and operational workflows
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including operations (SMOPS), security, connectivity, and internal customer teams


Qualifications

Skill requirements:


Background in Data Science, Software Engineering, or Data Engineering
Experience with Databases, preferably NoSQL systems
Understanding of REST APIs and JSON-based data structure
Advanced proficiency in Python development
Understanding ov GIT and CI/CD workflows
Kubernetes and Docker deployments
Basic Linux and Bash knowledge
Familiarity with cloud computing environments and services


Tools & Platforms


Python
ETL & Data Pipelines
Git
Bash
Clean Coding
CICD Automation GitOps
Linux
Kubernetes
Docker


Additional Information

What do we offer you?

Work environment & flexibility


International, dynamic and collaborative environment.
T-Social: social initiatives (sports, community, health, ...).
Hybrid work model (remote/on-site).
Flexible working hours.
Growth & development.
Customized training: access to Coursera to learn whatever you want, whenever you want.
Weekly language classes (English & German).
International Mentoring Sessions & Experience Days.


Compensation & Benefits


Flexible compensation plan (health insurance, meal vouchers, childcare, transport).
Telemedicine.
Life and accident insurance.
Social fund.


Wellbeing & time off


26+ working days of vacation per year
Free access to specialist services (medical, legal, wellness)
100% salary coverage during medical leave.


And many more advantages of being part of T-Systems!

If you are looking for a new challenge, do not hesitate to send us your CV! Please send CV in English. Join our team!

T-Systems Iberia will only process the CVs of candidates who meet the requirements specified for each offer.

Title: Applied Data Scientist
Description:
Overview

Keysight is at the forefront of technology innovation, delivering breakthroughs and trusted insights in electronic design, simulation, prototyping, test, manufacturing, and optimization. Our ~16,800 employees create world-class solutions in communications, 5G, automotive, energy, quantum, aerospace, defense, and semiconductor markets for customers in over 100 countries. Learn more about what we do.

Our award-winning culture embraces a bold vision of where technology can take us and a passion for tackling challenging problems with industry-first solutions. We believe that when people feel a sense of belonging, they can be more creative, innovative, and thrive at all points in their careers.

As an Applied Data Scientist, you’ll operate at the intersection of data engineering, data science, and machine learning. You’ll design and implement large-scale data architectures, develop robust data pipelines, and build high-quality ML models that integrate simulation and measurement data from diverse domains.

Your work will directly influence Keysight’s advanced R&D initiatives, from algorithm development to AI-assisted engineering tools.

Responsibilities


Partner with internal engineering and data teams to identify key data sources, define feature requirements, and align data standards across organizations.
Design, implement, and maintain data lakes, databases, and ETL/ELT pipelines (Snowflake, Databricks, SQL, Python).
Integrate, clean, and align simulation, measurement, and operational data for scalable AI/ML model development.
Conduct exploratory data analysis, dimensionality reduction (e.g., PCA), clustering, and regression to extract insights.
Develop and validate ML models using tree-based methods (XGBoost, LightGBM, Random Forests) and Bayesian Optimization for tuning.
Apply signal processing and data augmentation techniques to improve data quality and coverage.
Document data lineage, feature definitions, and modeling rationale for reproducibility and transparency.
Communicate insights and recommendations to stakeholders, influencing data-driven decisions across R&D and product teams.


Qualifications


Master’s or PhD in Data Science, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Statistics, or related field.
1+ years’ experience as a Data Scientist / Applied Data Scientist
Proven ability to build and maintain scalable data infrastructures (data lakes, schemas, pipelines).
Strong programming skills in Python (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn), SQL, and optionally C++.
Proficiency with Snowflake, Databricks, or similar big-data environments.
Hands-on expertise in tree-based ML techniques and statistical modeling.
Familiarity with Bayesian Optimization and feature engineering for time-series or signal data.
Ability to move fluidly between data exploration, engineering, and modeling tasks.


Desired Qualifications


Experience in data architecture design, schema governance, or cross-team data standards.
Knowledge of MLOps principles for productionizing models and maintaining pipelines.
Experience with metadata management and feature store design.
Prior exposure to environments combining simulation and real-world measurement data.


Careers Privacy Statement

Keysight Technologies Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other protected categories under all applicable laws.

The level of role and salary will be based on applicable experience, education and skills; Most offers will be between the minimum and the midpoint of the Salary Range listed below.

California Pay Range: MIN $117,750- MAX $196,250

Colorado Pay Range: MIN $109,030 - MAX $181,720

Note: For other locations, pay ranges will vary by region.

US Employees May Be Eligible For The Following Benefits


Medical, dental and vision
Health Savings Account
Health Care and Dependent Care Flexible Spending Accounts
Life, Accident, Disability insurance
Business Travel Accident and Business Travel Health
401(k) Plan
Flexible Time Off, Paid Holidays
Paid Family Leave
Discounts, Perks
Tuition Reimbursement
Adoption Assistance
ESPP (Employee Stock Purchase Plan)

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Buscamos un/a Científico/a de Datos altamente cualificado/a y con una sólida trayectoria en el desarrollo e integración de modelos de machine learning para unirse a nuestro equipo.

Si te apasiona el mundo de los datos, tienes experiencia demostrada en diseño, entrenamiento y validación de modelos de aprendizaje automático y profundo, y buscas un entorno dinámico donde puedas aplicar y expandir tus conocimientos, ¡esta es tu oportunidad!

Tus Responsabilidades Clave:


Diseñar, entrenar y validar modelos de machine learning y deep learning de vanguardia, asegurando su robustez y rendimiento.
Participar activamente en proyectos que abarquen diversas técnicas de modelado, incluyendo clustering, clasificación, regresión y detección de anomalías, para extraer conocimiento valioso de conjuntos de datos complejos.
Aplicar técnicas avanzadas de Procesamiento del Lenguaje Natural (NLP) para la extracción de información, resumen automático y análisis semántico, desbloqueando el potencial de los datos textuales.
Integrar modelos desarrollados en pipelines productivos eficientes y escalables, garantizando su correcta implementación y funcionamiento en entornos reales.
Liderar y ejecutar estudios de viabilidad y pruebas de concepto (POC) para explorar y validar nuevos casos de uso y oportunidades de negocio basadas en inteligencia artificial.
Documentar de manera exhaustiva las soluciones desarrolladas, incluyendo metodologías, resultados y especificaciones técnicas, facilitando la comprensión y adopción por parte de otros equipos.
Colaborar estrechamente con equipos multidisciplinares, incluyendo ingenieros de datos, desarrolladores y analistas de negocio, para asegurar la alineación de los proyectos con los objetivos estratégicos de la empresa y del cliente.
Participar en la formación y capacitación de usuarios finales y equipos técnicos, transfiriendo conocimiento y fomentando la cultura de datos dentro de la organización.
Contribuir activamente en la fase de pruebas de concepto (POC), validaciones funcionales y despliegues en entornos cloud como Azure y Google Cloud, aplicando las mejores prácticas de la industria.


Requisitos:

¿Qué Buscamos en Ti?


Formación universitaria superior en Informática, Matemáticas, Estadística, Física, Ingeniería o un campo relacionado. Se valorará Máster o Doctorado en Ciencia de Datos, Inteligencia Artificial o áreas afines.
Experiencia profesional demostrable de al menos 5 años como Científico/a de Datos o en un rol similar, con un enfoque claro en el desarrollo e integración de modelos de machine learning y deep learning.
Profundo conocimiento de algoritmos de machine learning (supervisados y no supervisados), incluyendo clustering, clasificación, regresión, y técnicas de reducción de dimensionalidad.
Experiencia práctica en el desarrollo de modelos de deep learning utilizando frameworks como TensorFlow, PyTorch o Keras.
Dominio de lenguajes de programación como Python (preferiblemente) o R, y experiencia con librerías relevantes para ciencia de datos (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, NLTK, SpaCy, etc.).
Experiencia en la aplicación de técnicas de NLP para el análisis de texto.
Conocimiento y experiencia en la integración de modelos en entornos de producción y en el ciclo de vida del desarrollo de software (MLOps).
Familiaridad con plataformas cloud (Azure, Google Cloud) y sus servicios relacionados con machine learning y análisis de datos.
Capacidad para realizar pruebas de concepto (POC) y estudios de viabilidad de manera autónoma.
Excelentes habilidades de comunicación, tanto oral como escrita, para explicar conceptos técnicos complejos a audiencias diversas.
Capacidad analítica, resolución de problemas y atención al detalle.
Proactividad, autonomía y una fuerte orientación a resultados.


¿Por qué Unirte a Metrica España?


Oportunidad de trabajar en proyectos retadores e innovadores con tecnologías de vanguardia.
Entorno de trabajo colaborativo y dinámico, con un equipo de profesionales apasionados por la tecnología.


¡Esperamos conocerte!

  • Dominio de lenguajes de programación como Python (preferiblemente) o R, y experiencia con librerías relevantes para ciencia de datos (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, NLTK, SpaCy, etc.).
  • Experiencia en la aplicación de técnicas de NLP para el análisis de texto.
  • Conocimiento y experiencia en la integración de modelos en entornos de producción y en el ciclo de vida del desarrollo de software (MLOps).
  • Familiaridad con plataformas cloud (Azure, Google Cloud) y sus servicios relacionados con machine learning y análisis de datos.
  • Capacidad para realizar pruebas de concepto (POC) y estudios de viabilidad de manera autónoma.
  • Excelentes habilidades de comunicación, tanto oral como escrita, para explicar conceptos técnicos complejos a audiencias diversas.
  • Capacidad analítica, resolución de problemas y atención al detalle.
  • Proactividad, autonomía y una fuerte orientación a resultados.

¿Por qué Unirte a Metrica España?

  • Oportunidad de trabajar en proyectos retadores e innovadores con tecnologías de vanguardia.
  • Entorno de trabajo colaborativo y dinámico, con un equipo de profesionales apasionados por la tecnología.

¡Esperamos conocerte!

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
¿Quiénes somos?

Somos una compañía tecnológica especializada en Inteligencia Artificial, IA Generativa, Analytics, Gobierno del Dato, Cloud y arquitecturas Event Driven, pero sobre todo somos un equipo con conocimiento, criterio y muchas ganas de hacer las cosas bien.

Trabajamos con talento altamente especializado, esté donde esté, y apostamos por una forma de crecer basada en la confianza, la autonomía y la participación. Nos mueve la excelencia tecnológica, la orientación a negocio y el sentido común, pero también el entusiasmo, la creatividad y la convicción de que la tecnología necesita alma, propósito y personas capaces de convertir la complejidad en soluciones reales.




Buscamos a un/a Data Scientist con 3-4 años de experiencia que quiera formar parte de proyectos de forecasting, clasificación, optimización y pircing para un cliente nacional destacado en el sector de las utilities.




¿Qué buscamos?

Experiencia sólida en Python.
Buen dominio de la modularización y la programación orientada a objetos.
Experiencia trabajando con Git y buenas prácticas de control de versiones.
Conocimiento en tests unitarios y prácticas de CI/CD.
Se valorará positivamente experiencia con Azure Databricks y Azure Machine Learning (Jobs, Pipelines, YAMLs)
Se valorará experiencia con PySpark.
Familiaridad con herramientas de gestión de tareas como Azure DevOps, Jira o similar
Buen nivel de inglés, tanto oral como escrito,




¿Cómo será tu día a día?

Extraerás y analizarás datos de las bases de datos para impulsar la optimización y la mejora del desarrollo de productos de la Segunda Ola Digital.
Evaluarás la eficacia y precisión de las nuevas fuentes de datos y aplicar técnicas de recopilación de datos.
Desarrollarás modelos personalizados para resolver las iniciativas de IA existentes a partir de los datos disponibles y haciendo uso de algoritmos.
Utilizarás el modelado predictivo para aumentar y optimizar las experiencias de los clientes, la generación de ingresos, la segmentación de anuncios y otros resultados empresariales.
Diseñarás marcos de prueba A / B y determinar siempre una métrica para evaluar los modelos.
Te coordinarás con diferentes equipos funcionales para implementar modelos y monitorizar los resultados.
Desarrollarás procesos y herramientas para supervisar y analizar el rendimiento del modelo y la precisión de los datos.




¿Qué ofrecemos?

Pertenecer a una empresa a la vanguardia tecnológica y centrado en las Personas, ponemos al trabajador en el centro de todo lo que hacemos. Creemos en una cultura horizontal, sin egos, en un entorno donde se valoran tanto las ideas como las personas.

Contrato indefinido y salario entre los 36.000 y 40.000 euros brutos/año de entrada, ajustado según tu experiencia (nuestro plan de carrera incluye 2 revisiones anuales). También tendrás a tu disposición un plan de formación especializado, participación en la propiedad y los beneficios de Mática: Wetaca gratuito en la oficina dos días a la semana, horario flexible con facilidades para trabajar desde donde quieras, seguro de salud, retribución flexible con Cobee, además de las vacaciones habituales, ofrecemos una semana extra en Navidad y tres días adicionales en Semana Santa. También recibirás un divertido Welcome Kit con portátil (podrás escoger entre varios modelos Mac o Windows), móvil, botella personalizada, auriculares bluetooth y golosinas.

Cuando estés en nuestra oficina, en el centro de Barcelona o Madrid, la fruta, los cafés y los refrescos corren a cuenta de Mática. ¡Ah! Y si eres amante de los animales, te gustará saber esto: ¡nuestras oficinas son Pet-friendly!




Si te interesa, escríbenos por LinkedIn o envía tu CV a seleccion@maticapartners.com




Do you want to be Mática?




Be Mática!

Title: #43 - AI Engineeer
Place: Castellón de la Plana, Valencian Community, Spain
Description:
Sobre nosotros

Imagine Software es una empresa de desarrollo de software especializada en soluciones ERP y productos tecnológicos a medida. Formamos parte del grupo Manbau y trabajamos en proyectos con impacto real para distintos sectores. Somos un equipo pequeño, técnico y con mucho recorrido por delante.

El rol

Buscamos un perfil orientado a Data Science e Inteligencia Artificial para unirse a nuestro equipo de R&D en Castellón. Trabajarás en la integración y desarrollo de capacidades de IA dentro de nuestros productos, colaborando con el equipo técnico en el ciclo completo de desarrollo.

Responsabilidades


Desarrollo e integración de modelos de IA y Machine Learning en productos propios
Conexión con APIs de IA (OpenAI, Azure AI, Hugging Face, etc.)
Análisis y procesamiento de datos para alimentar soluciones inteligentes
Colaboración con el equipo de R&D en decisiones técnicas y de arquitectura
Participación en el ciclo completo de desarrollo de producto



Requisitos


Experiencia en Data Science, Machine Learning o desarrollo de soluciones basadas en IA
Dominio de Python y librerías estándar (scikit-learn, pandas, NumPy, TensorFlow o PyTorch)
Experiencia con APIs de IA — OpenAI, Azure AI, o similares
Capacidad para trabajar de forma autónoma y en equipo
Incorporación presencial en Castellón



Valoramos


Experiencia con .NET o desarrollo Full Stack
Conocimiento de arquitecturas de microservicios o APIs REST
Experiencia con Azure o servicios cloud de Microsoft
Experiencia en entornos de producto ERP o software de gestión empresarial
Perfil proactivo con ganas de crecer técnica y profesionalmente



Qué ofrecemos


Incorporación a un equipo técnico consolidado con proyectos de largo recorrido
Ambiente de trabajo cercano y colaborativo
Crecimiento profesional real — valoramos y premiamos a quienes crecen con nosotros
Posición estable con proyección dentro del grupo



Otros datos


Nombre del cliente: Imagine Software
Modalidad de trabajo: Onsite por el momento.
Zona y franja horaria: Lunes a Jueves horario full time, Viernes intensivo. (Luego le comentamos en especifico)
Locación: Castellón
Idiomas: Español, inglés deseable
Tipo de contrato: Permanente. Full time
Rango salarial: No definido.

Title: Junior Data Scientist (m/f/d)
Description:
Join Alphabots Technology S.L.

 

Alphabots Technology S.L., a subsidiary of Alphabots GmbH headquartered in Munich, Germany, is looking for a talented Junior Data Scientist (m/f/d) to join our growing team in Valencia, Spain.

 

We develop innovative AI driven and automation based solutions that help organizations optimize business processes, unlock the value of data, and accelerate digital transformation.

If you are passionate about Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, and Intelligent Automation, and would like to contribute to exciting projects in an international environment, we would love to hear from you.

Are you excited about working on innovative AI projects and already have some hands-on experience in Artificial Intelligence or Machine Learning?

Do you enjoy solving complex problems through analytical thinking, a structured approach, and strong technical skills?

Then you are exactly who we are looking for.

 

Who We Are Looking For

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related field
Strong programming skills in Python
Fluent English and good German language skills
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and process oriented thinking
High attention to detail and a structured working style
Solid understanding of IT systems, software development, and business processes
Experience with Machine Learning and Deep Learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Keras
Initial hands-on experience in Machine Learning or Deep Learning projects gained through academic, personal, or professional work
Knowledge of data analysis, data preparation, and model evaluation techniques
Interest in AI-driven automation and Robotic Process Automation (RPA)
Strong communication and teamwork skills
Ability to work independently and take ownership of assigned tasks

 

What You Will Do

Advise and support clients in the digitalization and automation of business processes
Assist in the design, development, and implementation of Machine Learning and Deep Learning solutions
Analyze, prepare, and interpret structured and unstructured datasets
Train, evaluate, and optimize Machine Learning models for real-world applications
Automate business and operational workflows using AI and automation technologies
Support the development and implementation of Robotic Process Automation (RPA) solutions
Develop innovative methods for processing and analyzing specialized data types
Collaborate with experienced data scientists, software developers, and project managers on client projects
Research and evaluate emerging AI technologies, tools, and methodologies
Create innovative AI-driven solutions to automate complex business processes and improve operational efficiency
Prepare technical documentation and project reports

 

Why Alphabots?

Work with Cutting Edge Technology

Gain hands-on experience with the latest developments in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Generative AI, and Intelligent Automation technologies.

 

Make a Global Impact

Your work will help organizations across different industries solve complex challenges, improve efficiency, and create smarter business solutions.

 

Join an Exceptional Team

Become part of a team of passionate, creative professionals who thrive on collaboration, innovation, and excellence.

 

Grow Without Limits

We invest in your professional development through continuous learning opportunities, training programs, certifications, and challenging projects that help you reach your full potential.

 

Work on Meaningful Projects

Contribute to innovative AI and automation initiatives that deliver measurable business value and drive digital transformation.

 

Build the Future with Us

At Alphabots, we believe that Artificial Intelligence is more than technology it's a powerful tool to create meaningful change and shape the future of business.

 

What We Offer

Permanent full time employment
Competitive compensation package
Modern office in Valencia, Spain
International and dynamic working environment
Access to state of the art AI and automation technologies
Continuous training and professional development opportunities
Exciting national and international projects
Flat hierarchies and short decision-making processes
Career growth opportunities within a rapidly expanding AI company
A collaborative and supportive team culture

 

Apply Now

Become part of Alphabots Technology S.L. and help shape the future of Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, and Intelligent Automation.

Title: Data Scientist / Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Data Scientist / Machine Learning Engineer to join an AI project.




Responsibilities

Design and implement a machine learning algorithm that learns from user selections (e.g., choosing A from a set of candidates A, B, C, D) to improve future predictions.
Develop an efficient solution to retrieve the most relevant candidates from a dataset containing millions of records.
Build, test, and optimize models for accuracy and performance.
Collaborate with the development team to deploy the solution into production.

Requirements

3+ years of experience in Data Science or Machine Learning.
Strong Python skills and experience with machine learning frameworks.
Experience with large-scale datasets and search/retrieval techniques.
Knowledge of recommendation systems, ranking algorithms, or similar ML applications.
Experience deploying ML models into production is a plus.

Title: Climate Data Scientist
Description:
About Us

Climate change is creating unprecedented challenges for businesses, governments, and communities worldwide. At Mitiga Solutions, we help organizations understand, anticipate, and manage climate-related risks using science and technology.

Born from the Barcelona Supercomputing Center, we combine climate science, advanced modelling, and AI to turn complex data into actionable insights—supporting decisions from real-time forecasting to long-term climate projections. Our mission is simple: empower organizations to build resilience and adapt.

We seek someone to design and develop the models behind our new Energy product, delivering climate risk information for renewable energy and investment decisions. You can translate physical climate risks linked to atmospheric processes (e.g., temperature extremes) into climate risk settings—accounting for the details of physical processes and the uncertainty in projections. You communicate physical climate-related atmospheric risk to clients, both on its own and alongside other climate perils.

What You’ll Do


Contribute to the technical ownership of Mitiga’s physical climate-related atmospheric hazard models, collaborating with the product and science teams to define the development roadmap for our physical climate risk products.
Lead work to further validate our in-house atmospheric hazard models.
Lead the exploration and evaluation of 3rd party datasets that could complement our current offering, including possible integration into our current technology.
Work with our business and risk teams on appropriate ways to incorporate physical climate-related atmospheric risk into our ratings and impact calculations.
Be the ‘go-to’ expert for questions about physical climate-related atmospheric hazardsboth within the company and with our customers.


What We're Looking For


Minimum 4 years of experience in atmospheric hazard modelling, either Academic or Industry: At least 2 years of industry experience applying climate risk models and risk assessment methodologies.
Technical Expertise: Possess a deep understanding of multi-scale atmospheric hazard modelling. Experience of hands-on atmospheric hazard modelling, including statistical modelling of extremes (e.g. extreme value theory)
Climate Change Impact: Demonstrate an understanding of how climate change factors can influence physical related risks and atmospheric hazard dynamics and extremes.
Proven Delivery: Experience of successfully delivering atmospheric hazard risk analyses to clients, ensuring clear insights.
Hands-on Approach: Actively participate in the modelling process, contributing alongside our close-knit team. Skills in Python (or similar) and machine learning are highly desired.
Data-Driven Approach: Possess a strong analytical mind and utilize data effectively to inform decision-making.
Effective Communication: Have excellent stakeholder communication skills, enabling you to translate complex technical information into clear and concise messaging for diverse audiences.
Problem-Solving Skills: Demonstrate the ability to navigate ambiguous project requirements and develop clear solutions that drive the project forward.


We are open to a range of backgrounds. What matters most is drive, intellectual curiosity, and a genuine interest in climate or infrastructure.

Why work at Mitiga Solutions?


Purpose-driven impact. Your work will help shape resilience strategies for communities and critical infrastructure. From wildfires to windstorms, our models support decisions that matter.
Collaborative & inclusive culture. Work in multidisciplinary teams where transparency, rigour, and shared ownership are valued. Diverse perspectives are not only welcome, they are necessary.
Flexible working. Our hybrid model supports deep focus and balance. Enjoy compressed Fridays, 4-day summer weeks, your birthday off, and more!
State-of-the-art technology. We combine high-performance computing, proprietary modelling platforms, and machine learning to deliver accurate, high-resolution climate risk forecasts.
Flexible remuneration via Cobee. Get a monthly allowance for meals and maximize your salary with significant tax savings on transportation, learning, and more.

  • Collaborative & inclusive culture. Work in multidisciplinary teams where transparency, rigour, and shared ownership are valued. Diverse perspectives are not only welcome, they are necessary.
  • Flexible working. Our hybrid model supports deep focus and balance. Enjoy compressed Fridays, 4-day summer weeks, your birthday off, and more!
  • State-of-the-art technology. We combine high-performance computing, proprietary modelling platforms, and machine learning to deliver accurate, high-resolution climate risk forecasts.
  • Flexible remuneration via Cobee. Get a monthly allowance for meals and maximize your salary with significant tax savings on transportation, learning, and more.

Title: Expert Data Science
Description:
Summary

Location: Barcelona, Spain

Relocation Support: This role is based in Barcelona, Spain. Novartis is unable to offer relocation support: please only apply if accessible.

As an Expert Data Science within Novartis Digital Finance, you will build innovative analytics and AI solutions that help transform planning, forecasting, performance management, and decision-making across a global organization. Working at the intersection of Finance, data science, machine learning, and emerging AI technologies, you will turn complex data into meaningful insights, develop scalable solutions with measurable business impact, and help drive the next generation of intelligent Finance capabilities. If you are passionate about solving complex challenges, delivering real business value, and exploring the possibilities of Generative AI and advanced analytics, this is an opportunity to make a meaningful difference.

About The Role

Key Responsibilities


Design and deploy data science and artificial intelligence solutions that enhance finance planning and decision-making.
Develop predictive, prescriptive, and machine learning models that generate actionable business insights.
Transform complex business challenges into scalable analytics products, models, dashboards, and recommendations.
Collaborate with Finance stakeholders to identify high-value opportunities and drive solution adoption.
Prepare, validate, and optimize enterprise datasets to support analytics, reporting, and artificial intelligence initiatives.
Implement model monitoring, governance, and Responsible Artificial Intelligence practices across solution lifecycles.
Contribute to Generative Artificial Intelligence, large language model, retrieval-augmented generation, and Agentic Artificial Intelligence initiatives.


Essential Requirements


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
2–5 years of experience in data science, machine learning, advanced analytics, or applied artificial intelligence.
Proficiency in Python and SQL for data analysis, model development, and scalable solution delivery.
Strong knowledge of statistics, predictive modeling, forecasting, machine learning, and model evaluation techniques.
Experience translating business requirements into analytical solutions with measurable business impact.
Ability to communicate complex analytical findings clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders.


Desirable Requirements


Experience applying data science, forecasting, planning, or performance analytics within Finance or similar business functions.
Exposure to Generative Artificial Intelligence, large language models, retrieval-augmented generation, or Agentic Artificial Intelligence frameworks.


We are looking to fill 2 roles.
