  • Managing data ingestion, orchestration, transformation, quality, and curation at scale.
  • Implementing and maintaining systems via event-driven architectures and APIs.
  • Collaborating closely with Data Scientists, product teams, and business stakeholders to translate data needs into robust solutions.
  • Ensuring high engineering standards: unit testing, mocking, CI/CD, code quality, repository structure, environment setup.
  • Providing technical guidance, code reviews, and mentoring to team members.

In order to succeed, you’ll need

  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, software engineering, or comparable work experience
  • Proficiency in Python, including automation, scripting, and data engineering frameworks
  • Experience in ML/GenAI projects
  • Strong experience with AWS, particularly: S3, Lambda, Step Functions, SQS/SNS, EventBridge, CloudWatch, IAM, DynamoDB, batch processing (Glue Jobs/Workflows, Batch). Other cloud providers are also welcomed
  • Hands-on experience with Infrastructure as Code (preferably AWS CDK)
  • Strong experience with Spark/PySpark or similar distributed processing frameworks
  • Proficiency with Git, repo structure management, Docker, testing (unit tests, mocking, isolation)
  • Practical knowledge of CI/CD pipelines (GitLab/GitHub Actions/CircleCI)
  • Experience building scalable, production-grade data pipelines
  • Great English skills, both written and spoken.

Nice to have

  • Experience with streaming systems (Kafka, Kinesis)
  • Experience with dashboards or visualization tools (Quicksight, Streamlit, Dash)

Company Benefits

  • 1200€ to equip your home office
  • Flex allowance: 720€/year (60€ per month x12)
  • Performance bonus
  • Health insurance extendable to your household
  • Wellbeing program extendable to your household
  • Life insurance
  • Pension fund
  • Smartphone & phone data plan
  • Discounts on VW Group cars and special financing conditions

Cool Things You’ll Experience In Our Work Environment

  • Tailored remote-friendly setup for every team's vibe!
  • Flexible working hours
  • Multicultural reality with + 30 different nationalities
  • Pet friendly environment
  • Boost your career with our internal mobility program, focused on your personal & professional growth
  • Mix and mingle across teams for idea-sharing and bonding

About VW Group Digital Solutions [Portugal]

Technology-driven trends such as diverse mobility, electrification and connectivity will revolutionize the mobility industry in the next decade. Volkswagen Group, as world leader in the automotive industry, is setting the pace for this revolution.

At Volkswagen Group Digital Solutions [Portugal] we are the go-to team to tackle complex systems and programming challenges. We make sure that our products flow smoothly in all stages of the digital value chain.

Our main areas of focus: vehicle software & operations, mobile online services, IT security, fleet management, sales & after sales, purchasing & logistics and product portfolio management.

Ready to #ExploreNewRoads? Join us! 🚗

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: GenAI Analyst
Description:
About AUTOMAISE

AUTOMAISE is based in Braga, Portugal and is developing an Automated Machine Learning platform to build productivity solutions for Operations and Customer Care. We offer a no-code/low-code platform that enables brands to automate complex processes within incredibly short timeframes, helping organizations supercharge digital transformation strategies.

Job Overview:

We're looking for a Generative AI Analyst with a focus on enhancing our chatbot and voicebot solutions. This role is ideal for someone passionate about improving AI interactions and making digital communication feel more human. You'll work on refining the conversational abilities of our AI by testing, iterating, and optimizing prompts and knowledge bases.

What you’ll do:


Iteratively fine-tune GenAI prompts and parameters and adjust knowledge bases to enhance conversation quality and user experience.
Perform quality analysis of chatbot interaction logs, prompts, enabling continuous improvement of conversational accuracy and user experience.
Apply basic Python skills for data analysis and solutions testing.
Collaborate with the AI team to implement changes that improve bot performance and customer satisfaction.
Conduct thorough testing of our chatbot and voicebot solutions to identify areas for improvement.
Analyze user feedback and interaction data to guide your optimization efforts.
Ensure our bots' language capabilities are inclusive, accurate, and culturally relevant, with particular emphasis on Portuguese and English.


Minimum Requirements

What we’re looking for:


2 years minimum of experience in a data analyst role and an academic degree in a Computer Science field, Mathematics or Statistics.
Strong analytical skills, with a keen eye for detail and a creative problem-solving approach.
Familiarity with Large Language Models (LLMs) (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, etc.) and their strengths/limitations.
Ability to design, test, and iterate prompts systematically.
Understanding of prompt engineering principles (e.g., role prompting, chain-of-thought structuring, few-shot examples, and evaluation strategies).
Basic knowledge of Python for data handling and analysis.
Exposure to vector databases, embeddings, or retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and communicate effectively across different departments.
Passion for AI and a user-centric approach to technology development.
Basic understanding of APIs is a plus, but not required.
Experience with chatbot or voicebot platforms is a plus, but not required.
Excellent command of Portuguese and English.


What you’ll get from us:


Competitive Salary: Attractive compensation based on your experience and role.
Generous Time Off: Enjoy 22 days of paid annual leave plus 5 extra days.
Flexible Work Options: Hybrid work regime.
Performance Bonuses: Earn bonuses based on your achievements.
Health Benefits: Comprehensive health insurance coverage.
Career Growth: Opportunities for personal and professional development.
Continuous Learning: Access to training programs to enhance your skills.
Supportive Team: Join a dynamic, agile, and expanding team.
Growth Opportunities: Thrive in a growing company with a strong career path.
Positive Environment: Enjoy a collaborative and open work culture.
Unique Office Space: Work in an inspiring office at Braga’s city center with a historic medieval wall backdrop.
Work-Life Balance: Embrace a balanced work-life with flexibility and support.
Fun Perks: Engage in company events, treats, and team-building activities.


Ready to Take the Next Step? APPLY NOW!

We are excited to hear everything about you and why you think this role is a fit for you. Please reach out with your LinkedIn profile/CV, and don’t hesitate to contact us with any questions at jobs@automaise.com

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 We're Hiring | Python Developer – Risk Analytics & Process Automation




Are you passionate about Python, Data Analytics, and Financial Risk? We're looking for a Python Developer to join an international team focused on building innovative digital solutions that transform risk management processes.

Working alongside risk specialists and technology teams, you'll develop automation tools, interactive dashboards, and data-driven applications that improve efficiency, reduce operational risk, and support better business decision-making.




What you'll do

Design and develop Python-based solutions to automate risk management processes.
Analyse and re-engineer existing workflows to improve efficiency and user experience.
Build data processing tools and analytics solutions using modern Python libraries.
Collaborate closely with risk analysts to translate business requirements into technical solutions.
Develop dashboards, APIs, and automation tools following Agile and software engineering best practices.
Work with large datasets to generate meaningful insights and support decision-making.
Contribute to the continuous evolution of digital solutions used by international teams.




What we're looking for

Master's degree in Finance, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
2–5 years of experience in Financial Services, Risk Management, Data Analytics, or a similar environment.
Strong programming skills in Python.
Solid experience with:
Pandas
NumPy
Data analysis and data manipulation
Experience analysing large datasets and transforming business requirements into technical solutions.
Knowledge of Market Risk, Counterparty Risk, or Cross-Margining is highly valued.
Experience working in Agile environments is a plus.




Nice to have

Experience developing dashboards, APIs, or automation tools.
Familiarity with HTML, CSS, or JavaScript.
Interest in AI, process automation, or data-driven applications.




Soft skills

✔ Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities

✔ Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

✔ Collaborative mindset and team spirit

✔ Curious, proactive, and eager to learn

✔ Strong organisational and prioritisation skills

✔ Client-focused with attention to detail and quality




Language

Fluent English (mandatory)




Why join?

Work on innovative projects combining Python, Data Analytics, Automation, and Financial Risk.
Collaborate with international teams in a highly technical and business-focused environment.
Gain exposure to modern technologies, Agile practices, and AI-driven initiatives.
Make a real impact by developing tools that support critical risk management decisions.




📩 Interested? Apply now or send me a direct message to learn more about this opportunity.

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
We are looking for an AI Engineer to join our team at JTA. In this role, you will help shape our Data Science & AI department by designing, building, and deploying intelligent systems end to end, from early research and prototyping to production services, evaluation, and continuous improvement.




You will play an important role in the R&D of cutting-edge solutions, with a strong focus on LLMs and GenAI, while also contributing across the broader engineering lifecycle of AI systems. This is a hands-on role for someone with strong engineering fundamentals, practical experience building AI systems, and the ability to learn and adapt quickly in a fast-moving space.




What you will be doing:




Contribute to the vision and growth of JTA’s Data Science & AI department.
Design, build, and deploy end-to-end AI solutions, with a strong focus on LLMs, GenAI systems, and intelligent automation.
Work across a diverse set of challenges, from LLM-based applications and agentic workflows to recommendation systems and other applied ML use cases.
Collaborate closely with product, business, and technical stakeholders to translate strategic goals into scalable AI solutions.
Explore, research, and prototype new ideas that help bridge the gap between academia and industry, driving forward the R&D of innovative approaches.
Take part in the full lifecycle of AI systems, from ideation and experimentation to deployment, monitoring, iteration, and long-term maintenance.
Build reliable services and application logic around AI systems, ensuring clean interfaces, robustness, and production readiness.
Contribute to system design, architecture discussions, and code reviews, ensuring high standards of scalability, maintainability, and performance.
Develop and improve evaluation workflows, testing practices, and observability for AI systems in production.
Work across data-intensive workflows and operational pipelines to ensure reliable inputs, outputs, and integration with existing platforms.
Collaborate closely with infrastructure and platform teams to integrate AI systems into existing development and delivery workflows.
Support and mentor junior team members, helping to grow their technical capabilities and engineering mindset.




Requirements:




Master’s or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related field.
2+ years of experience working at the interception of Artificial Intelligence and Computer Science with a strong foundation in Python.
Proven experience working with LLMs and/or agentic frameworks in production or applied R&D environments.
Solid understanding of the full lifecycle of AI/ML systems and how to bring them into production at scale.
Strong software engineering fundamentals, with the ability to write clean, modular, tested, and maintainable code.
Practical experience building and integrating production services, APIs, or backend components that support AI applications.
Hands-on experience with modern AI development tools, frameworks, and workflows.
Comfort working with version control, CI/CD, containerization, cloud platforms, and modern engineering practices.
Good understanding of system design, reliability, performance, and engineering trade-offs in production environments.
Balance of theoretical ML knowledge and hands-on implementation skills.
Ability to learn quickly, adapt to new technologies, and work effectively across evolving technical challenges.
Ability to work cross-functionally with other technical teams and communicate effectively with non-technical stakeholders.




Perks:




Annual gross salary between €30,000 and €40,000, depending on experience and qualifications, with room for flexibility;
Flexible Hybrid Work System;
15th Month Bonus Salary Policy;
4 extra vacation days;
Participation in International Projects;
Company-Paid Certifications;
Training, career progression and support;
Team Building Activities,
Innovative & Young Culture.

Title: Distributed Cloud | AI/Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description

We are seeking a versatile and passionate AI / Machine Learning Engineer to join our data science and engineering team. You will be instrumental in bridging the gap between data science research and production-ready applications, building scalable machine learning systems that drive business value. This role requires a strong balance of software engineering principles and ML expertise.

Key Responsibilities


Design, develop, and implement end-to-end Machine Learning pipelines for training, testing, and deployment of predictive models.
Work closely with Data Scientists to translate prototypes and models into scalable, production-grade code (MLOps).
Develop robust, efficient, and well-documented code primarily using Python and relevant ML frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn).
Implement monitoring and alerting solutions for models in production to track performance, detect drift, and ensure reliability.
Collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure efficient data preparation, feature engineering, and access to necessary data infrastructure.
Stay current with the latest advancements in AI, ML techniques, and scalable infrastructure.



Qualifications


3+ years of professional experience in a Machine Learning Engineer, AI Developer, or similar role.
Strong expertise in Python and object-oriented programming, with a focus on code quality and best practices.
Mandatory practical experience with major Machine Learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch) and libraries (e.g., scikit-learn).
Solid understanding of MLOps principles and the tools required for model deployment, versioning, and lifecycle management (e.g., MLflow, Kubeflow, or similar).
Proficiency in SQL and experience working with large datasets and data warehousing concepts.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to communicate complex technical concepts effectively.
Experience working with Cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) for model deployment, compute, and storage (e.g., SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML Services).
Knowledge of containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes) for building reproducible and scalable ML environments.
Familiarity with distributed computing frameworks (e.g., Spark).
Advanced degree (M.S. or Ph.D.) in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Very good level of English, both spoken and written, for effective communication with international teams;



Additional Information

The Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. HTML:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description

We are seeking a versatile and passionate AI / Machine Learning Engineer to join our data science and engineering team. You will be instrumental in bridging the gap between data science research and production-ready applications, building scalable machine learning systems that drive business value. This role requires a strong balance of software engineering principles and ML expertise.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, and implement end-to-end Machine Learning pipelines for training, testing, and deployment of predictive models.
  • Work closely with Data Scientists to translate prototypes and models into scalable, production-grade code (MLOps).
  • Develop robust, efficient, and well-documented code primarily using Python and relevant ML frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn).
  • Implement monitoring and alerting solutions for models in production to track performance, detect drift, and ensure reliability.
  • Collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure efficient data preparation, feature engineering, and access to necessary data infrastructure.
  • Stay current with the latest advancements in AI, ML techniques, and scalable infrastructure.


Qualifications

  • 3+ years of professional experience in a Machine Learning Engineer, AI Developer, or similar role.
  • Strong expertise in Python and object-oriented programming, with a focus on code quality and best practices.
  • Mandatory practical experience with major Machine Learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch) and libraries (e.g., scikit-learn).
  • Solid understanding of MLOps principles and the tools required for model deployment, versioning, and lifecycle management (e.g., MLflow, Kubeflow, or similar).
  • Proficiency in SQL and experience working with large datasets and data warehousing concepts.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to communicate complex technical concepts effectively.
  • Experience working with Cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) for model deployment, compute, and storage (e.g., SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML Services).
  • Knowledge of containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes) for building reproducible and scalable ML environments.
  • Familiarity with distributed computing frameworks (e.g., Spark).
  • Advanced degree (M.S. or Ph.D.) in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related quantitative field.
  • Very good level of English, both spoken and written, for effective communication with international teams;


Additional Information

The Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

Title: Analista de dados
Description:
Mutual Respect, Empathy, Innovation, and Professional Pride. Do you see yourself within these values?




Founded in 2006 with the mission "To Challenge Complexity", Closer Consulting is a Data Science company specializing in Business Intelligence, Advanced Analytics, and Artificial Intelligence.

Developing projects in the most diverse industries – Banking, Telecommunications, Healthcare, Retail, Energy, Insurance, and Aviation, among others –, we have created an environment where the brightest enquiring minds feel at home, providing our people with the means, opportunities and support they need to thrive and innovate.




We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst to take on new challenges in a hybrid work model in Porto.




Are you the one?




What You'll Do

Process Automation: Analyse, re-engineer, and automate complex risk management processes using Python, adhering to production-level coding standards.
Tool & App Development: Build interactive visualization tools, bots, and APIs to streamline risk decision-making and operational efficiency.
Cross-Margining Deliverables: Collaborate closely with risk managers to develop specialized tools for cross-margining and dynamic risk monitoring.
Production-Ready Coding: Work hand-in-hand with RISK IT to write clean, maintainable code directly within official production environments.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Translate complex user requirements from risk analysts into practical, high-impact digital solutions.

Required Skills

Education: Master’s degree in Finance, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Experience: 2 to 5 years of professional experience in financial markets, risk management, or quantitative data analysis.
Financial Domain Knowledge: Strong understanding of risk management concepts and familiarity with cross-margining.
Python Mastery: Solid working knowledge of Python focused on data analysis, with hands-on experience in Pandas, NumPy, and building APIs.
Engineering Best Practices: Familiarity with full-stack concepts (e.g. HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and modern software development practices.
Data & Analytics: Exceptional ability to process, simplify, and extract insights from large, structured datasets.
Communication: Expert level in English, with the ability to articulate complex concepts clearly to stakeholders and technical teams.




If you believe in this match as much as we do, send us your CV to lia.giannone@closer.pt and reach the closer side of life.




Closer is a company that values diversity and inclusion. We are committed to fostering an environment where everyone is treated with dignity and respect, regardless of race, gender, beliefs, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, or other personal characteristics.




At Closer, we embrace the unique perspectives and experiences that everyone brings, and we believe that it is a fundamental driver for the success we've achieved.

HTML:
Com mais de 8 anos de existência a nível nacional, contamos com mais de 200 colaboradores com o nosso ADN e posicionamo-nos no mercado com 3 ofertas de serviço:


  • Strategy (Outsourcing, Team as a Service, Tech Academies);
  • R&D (Estágios, POC, Laboratório Tecnológico);
  • Nearshore.


Na ADENTIS centramo-nos nas PESSOAS e é este o nosso salário emocional:


  • Equilíbrio entre a vida profissional e pessoal;
  • Flexibilidade na rotina organizacional;
  • Benefícios de saúde para colaboradores e familiares;
  • Eventos Team Building;
  • Protocolo com mais de 300 parceiros;
  • Formação contínua através de um centro profissional de formação e certificação;
  • Acompanhamento e feedback contínuo através de um plano personalizado;
  • Progressão de carreira através de avaliações objetivas e diretas.


IMPORTANTE: Exige-se fluência em português (falado e escrito) e autorização legal para trabalhar em Portugal.


O que procuramos:


  • Licenciatura em Engenharia Informática ou similar;
  • 3 anos de experiência profissional como Machine Learning Engineer;
  • Proeficiência em Python;
  • Experiência com AWS;
  • Bons conhecimentos de Pytorch;
  • Excelente nível de inglês falado e escrito.


Se queres fazer parte de uma empresa People First, junta-te a nós.


We walk with you!


Na Adentis defendemos a igualdade e valorizamos a diversidade. Criamos um ambiente seguro, diversificado e onde as oportunidades são iguais para todos os colaboradores!

Não discriminamos com base em idade, etnia, orientação sexual, género, deficiência ou qualquer fator que não seja o mérito.

Todas as candidaturas com competências para o cargo são bem-vindas!

Title: Data Engineer (English speaking) (M/F/D)
Description:
First insights

Data engineering is your passion, and you love to make sure that data can be turned into valuable assets? Are you keen on creating data products that enable teams and organizations to increase their productivity? Do you have solid experience in designing and maintaining data architectures? Do you have an engineering mindset and love to analyze complex datasets? Do you love to work in an agile environment and deliver in short iterations?

If this sounds interesting to you, then it is YOU we need in our team!

Location: Aveiro, Portugal

For this position, it is necessary to be available to come to the office at least 2x a week.

That’s What It’s About


Design, develop, optimize, and maintain data architecture.
Design and maintain ingestion of multiple data sources.
Analyze, manipulate, transform, and process large and complex datasets.
Enable training and running machine learning model.
Building real-time data pipelines.
Help our customers to become cloud-native and data-driven companies.
Support your team with active knowledge transfer.
Be part of and influence the introduction of new tools, methodologies, and techniques.
Work in an agile environment and cross-functional team.


This is you

At heart you are a passionate team player who respects the opinions of your colleagues, as:


You know how to be the best team player.
You have an eye for details and ace in documenting your work.
You base your decisions on metrics.
You are very structured, and you set the benchmark for quality.
You are open to new technologies.
You have at minimum 3 years of experience as a Data Engineer.
You have at minimum 3 years of experience in either Python or Scala and SQL.
You have a bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, or data engineering, or you have a relevant subject such as mathematics or physics.
You have experience in semantic modeling of complex data landscapes and are familiar with concepts of Data Lake, Data Warehouse, Data Vault, and Data Mart.
You have a deep understanding of various data stores, both structured and unstructured, and their capabilities (i.e. distributed filesystems, SQL, and NoSQL data stores).
You know exactly how to structure data pipelines for reliability, scalability, and optimal performance.
You are comfortable working with analytics processing engines (i.e. Spark, Flink).
You have worked with many different storage formats and know when to use which (i.e. JSON, Parquet, ORC).
You speak fluent English.


Bonus experience (nice to have):


ML Engineering & MLOps experience, including deploying, tracking, and monitoring models in production using MLFlow, Kubeflow, TensorFlow Serving, or similar tools.
Experience with cloud technologies such as Azure, Databricks, Fabric, Snowflake, AWS Athena, or Google BigQuery
Experience building real-time data pipelines using tools like Azure, Stream Analytics, Amazon Kinesis, Google Cloud Dataflow, Kafka, or RabbitMQ.
Familiarity with CI/CD for data pipelines and ML models, including tools such as GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or Airflow.


About Us

At MobiLab, we are committed to empowering our employees to bring their creative mindset into action, guiding our customers toward reaching their full data potential, and becoming Cloud-native organizations.

We are a diverse and dynamic team united by delivering engineering excellence. We are committed to inclusivity, where individuals of all backgrounds, including those with disabilities, feel welcomed and valued. You will directly impact how future business works and contribute to industry-leading companies.

We are dedicated to growing our employees. Our company culture encourages knowledge sharing and learning with a dedicated MobiLab Career Development. Our headquarters, located in the heart of Cologne, offers a creative work environment. We provide a range of benefits, including a public transport ticket, access to industry conferences, a company pension scheme, and more.

If you’re passionate about Cloud Integration and striving for engineering perfection, we invite you to join our MobiLab Team. Let’s grow together!

Title: AI Solutions Builder
Description:
About Smartcat

Smartcat is building the future of work, where human expertise meets digital teammates to drive 10x to 1000x productivity gains for the world’s leading enterprises.

We’re on the frontier of an entirely new category: Agentic AI. We enable enterprises to build high-performing hybrid workforces made up of both humans and AI agents. These AI agents aren’t generic copilots. They’re fully trained digital teammates that learn from your best people, your content, and your business strategy—ready to get to work from day one.

Our platform combines generative AI, human-in-the-loop workflows, and a living Enterprise Skill Graph that continuously learns and improves. Whether you're launching a product globally, onboarding new hires, translating learning content, or aligning legal teams across regions, Smartcat turns knowledge into action and action into scale.

Over 1,000 companies, including 20% of the Fortune 500, rely on Smartcat to bring their business to the world—instantly, accurately, and in every language. As a Series C company with consistently high year-over-year growth, we’re scaling fast and investing in people who want to shape the future of work with us.

Join us in unlocking global potential, one human and agent team at a time.

This role is ideal for someone who loves AI, thinks in systems, and enjoys building practical solutions that drive measurable impact.

You don't need to be a software engineer, but you should be comfortable working with modern AI tools, experimenting quickly, and learning fast. We're looking for strong curiosity, basic coding ability, and real "vibe coding" experience.

You'll work directly with the executive and leadership teams to build AI-powered tools and knowledge systems that improve how the company operates and makes decisions. You'll join a small, high-impact team within the Office of the CEO.

Responsibilities


Build and optimize AI-powered internal tools for:
Sales pipeline analysis and customer intelligence
Internal knowledge management
Operational reporting and business insights
Executive decision support
Work with structured and unstructured data, including vector databases, MCP, and RAG
Build and maintain AI agents that combine semantic and keyword search across internal knowledge bases, meeting transcripts, and CRM data
Manage infrastructure for AI services, with support from DevOps
Translate ambiguous business problems into scalable, AI-enabled solutions
Help drive AI adoption and AI-first thinking across the organization

Requirements


Strong interest in AI, automation, and emerging technologies
Familiarity with AI coding tools such as Claude, Cursor, Cline, or similar
Basic coding skills (Python), or strong motivation to learn quickly
Comfortable with the command line, basic Linux administration, and cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP)
Proven ability to drive projects end-to-end
Comfortable operating in a fast-moving startup environment with ambiguity
Strong communication and collaboration skills
Ability to turn business needs into practical AI tools
English and Russian proficiency required


What We Offer


Fully remote work
The chance to work at an AI-native company at the forefront of agentic AI
Direct collaboration with the CEO and executive team
High ownership and autonomy — your work directly influences company operations
A fast-paced, highly collaborative, and supportive team
A culture built on innovation, speed, ownership, and results
Room to grow alongside a rapidly scaling global company


Why joining Smartcat might be your best move so far


A global, connected team We’re a team of 200+ people across 30+ countries, working together through a hub-and-spoke model anchored in eight key locations: New York, London, Lisbon, Costa Rica, Serbia, Armenia, Georgia, and Spain. Most of our roles are remote-friendly, giving you the flexibility to do your best work from where you thrive. For customer-facing teams, we prioritize proximity to our customers, ensuring we stay deeply connected to their needs and deliver an exceptional experience. No matter where you’re based, you’ll be part of a highly collaborative, globally-distributed team that values ownership, speed, and meaningful impact.
Be part of an AI Native Organization We are highly innovative, using AI across all areas of the organization to accelerate decision-making and free people to focus on strategy and high-impact work. We embrace new ideas and encourage all Smartcaters, regardless of level or department, to manage their own AI Agents. At Smartcat you’ll shape how AI transforms the workplace and play an integral role in ensuring Smartcat remains a leader in AI innovation.
Innovating a $100 Billion industry Smartcat is reshaping the $100B multilingual content industry with an AI-powered platform that makes it easy for companies to create, translate, and localize global content at scale. Our platform enables enterprise teams to move away from slow, traditional outsourcing methods, and achieve fast, high-quality results, at a fraction of the cost.
Join the rocketship to scale-up 10x and beyond together We are looking for someone to become an integral part of our team and play a crucial role in the most exciting part of our journey: transitioning from a post-Series C startup to a company exceeding $100M in ARR and $1B in valuation. Our journey isn’t for the faint of heart. We are growing at 130% YoY, thanks to our strong product-market fit and high-performing team, and plan to accelerate from here.
Smartcat Culture Code: Where Diversity Meets High Performance At Smartcat, we are committed to building a culture that highlights respect and appreciation for each individual's unique background and perspective, while maintaining a strong focus on results and engagement. We believe in welcoming everyone and fostering an inclusive environment where team members can be their authentic selves at work. Our commitment to inclusion is steadfast, and we stand firmly against discrimination and harassment.

Title: AI Developer
Description:
We want to do different with the essential’s things.

We are committed to a sustainable corporatism strategy. We believe that success is based on building a team focused on collective well-being, personal and professional development, and based on a work philosophy that enhances the healthy management of our lives. Work-life balance, beginner’s mind and community involvement are our identity, our foundations, and our culture.




What you need to join us:




Master's degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Applied Mathematics, or a related field.
Experience developing AI systems, especially agentic AI or multi-agent systems.
Strong Python programming skills (C++ or Java is a plus).
Experience with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, or Scikit-learn.
Understanding of AI concepts such as perception, reasoning, planning, learning, and decision-making.
Experience with simulation environments like OpenAI Gym or Unity ML-Agents.
Familiarity with agent-based modeling tools such as NetLogo, JADE, or Mason.
Ability to work with large datasets and prepare them for training AI models.
Knowledge of Agile development methodologies.
Fluent French (required).
Technical English for reading documentation (preferred)







Important notes:

- It’s a hybrid project in Porto or Lisbon.







What do we have to offer you:




- Personalized Talent Management. We value and privilege a close and regular follow-up; we are a team from day one.

- Broad and flexible benefits package. Health insurance, life and personal accident insurance, discounts and benefits, telecommunications plan and much more.

- We invest in training and in your career. Certified training, online training platforms, Pearson VUE exam center... We bet on you!

- Sustainability and community involvement. We want to give meaning to our work and invest in an inclusive consulting project. For that, we need your help.




team.it, back to basics!

Will you join us?







At Team IT we defend equality and value diversity.

We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone!

We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability, or any factor other than merit.

All applications with skills for the position are welcome!

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
About Us

Planet is a leading technology company transforming payments by putting customer experience first. We offer integrated solutions that include payment processing, VAT refunds, dynamic currency conversion, and management services for merchants in the Retail and Hospitality sectors worldwide.

In recent years, we have experienced significant growth, expanding our services and global presence.

With strong private equity investors, Advent International and Eurazeo, we have the financial capital and expertise to grow our capabilities and reach through acquisitions.

Our mission is to create a world of connected commerce where payments are simple, secure, and seamless, enabling our partners to deliver exceptional experiences to their customers.

Role Overview

This role owns the design, delivery, and ongoing behaviour of production AI workflows that automate and augment work across Planet’s internal systems — HR, Finance, Operations, and CRM. You will be accountable not only for shipping the technology, but for the quality of the decisions it makes: building agents, prompts, and retrieval pipelines that business users can trust, and improving them over time through measurement and real-world feedback. The impact is direct — reliable, auditable AI that reduces manual effort and raises the quality and speed of everyday decisions across the business.

What You Will Do


Design and build production-grade AI workflows that integrate across internal systems (HR, Finance, Operations, CRM).
Implement the orchestration logic — triggers, retries, fallbacks, and human-in-the-loop patterns — so workflows run reliably, observably, and auditably.
Develop and maintain the AI agents, prompts, retrieval pipelines, and decision logic that power those workflows.
Own model behaviour in production: accuracy and usefulness, failure modes, and edge-case and ambiguity handling.
Iterate on model performance using evaluation frameworks, feedback loops, and real-world usage data.
Take accountability for decision quality and outcomes — not just technical execution — defining and tracking success metrics such as accuracy, resolution rate, and time saved.
Diagnose cases where systems are technically “working” but producing poor outcomes, and close the gap.
Partner with Data Engineering on shared platforms (infrastructure, CI/CD, data pipelines, security and governance) and contribute to shared standards, schemas, and best practices for AI systems.


Who You Are


Strong software engineering background (Python or similar), with a track record of building production systems — not just prototypes.
Hands-on experience with AI / ML systems (LLMs, classifiers, decision models, or similar).
Experience integrating APIs and working with distributed systems.
Experience designing prompts, retrieval pipelines, or ML inference workflows.
Solid understanding of model evaluation, monitoring, and feedback loops; comfortable working with both structured and unstructured data.
Product-oriented — cares about outcomes, not just shipping code; pragmatic about AI and focused on what works in production.
Comfortable owning ambiguity and making trade-offs.
Languages: English
A plus, but not required: workflow orchestration tools (Temporal, Airflow, Step Functions); experience building internal tools or agents; familiarity with regulated or enterprise environments; exposure to MLOps or AI evaluation frameworks.


Why Planet

Planet is an equal opportunity employer where diversity is valued, and all employment is decided based on qualifications, merit, and business need.

Come and grow your career in the most exciting, fast paced technology market, with a business that delivers feel-good connected commerce. We would love to hear from you – Apply now.

At Planet, we embrace a hybrid work model, with three days a week in the office.

Reasonable accommodations may be made in order to allow for an individual to perform the essential functions of this role successfully.

Title: Lead Data Scientist
Description:
Unleash transformative data solutions — drive innovation through scalable, cutting-edge pipelines!




We’re hiring a Lead Data Scientist to join a leading banking/financial services environment in Lisbon with hybrid work model (up to 3 remote days per week).




What you'll do:

Lead and technically guide a Data Scientists team and analytics professionals, ensuring the implementation of best practices.
Drive the adoption and integration of AI solutions across projects, fostering innovation and efficiency.
Oversee the development of Power BI dashboards and data science models, ensuring high quality and impactful insights.
Collaborate with stakeholders to define strategic goals and translate them into technical solutions.
Promote a culture of continuous improvement, knowledge sharing, and technical excellence within the team.
Ensure project deliverables meet deadlines, quality standards, and business objectives.
Mentor team members, supporting their professional growth and technical capabilities.
Stay current with industry trends in AI, data science, and BI tools to continuously enhance the team's skill set.




What we’re looking for:

Minimum 9 years of experience in data science, analytics, or related fields.
Proven leadership experience in leading technical teams, ideally in AI and data-driven projects.
Strong expertise in Power BI and data visualization best practices.
Solid understanding of AI/ML concepts and their application in real-world scenarios.
Experience implementing data science solutions within a corporate environment.




Language Required for the role:

Good communication skills in English (minimum B2 level).




Eligibility to work in the EU:

Only candidates with an existing legal right to work in Europe will be considered for this role.

Interested? Apply now and include your CV (preferably in English) along with a statement confirming your consent to the processing and storage of your personal data.

Title: Python Data Engineer
Description:
Join us as a Python Data Engineer

We are looking for a Python Data Engineer to drive the development and calculation of indices using the Dataiku platform. This position is based in Porto.

You will join an agile team embedded within the Index business, supporting the development of indices and related analytics, while expanding our data and analytical capabilities.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with business stakeholders and support projects end-to-end, from ideation and rapid prototyping to production deployment, ensuring that innovation and reliability go hand in hand across our data ecosystem.

Why this role is exciting


Build scalable data solutions that support index development and analytics
Work with large and complex datasets using Python, pandas and Dataiku
Collaborate closely with business stakeholders and cross-functional teams
Influence the performance and reliability of production data pipelines
Contribute to a growing, innovative team in the Index business


Key Responsibilities


Write clean, maintainable, and robust code following best practices
Evaluate project feasibility and technical complexity, prioritizing code quality and robustness
Design optimized data processing flows for scalability and performance
Conduct peer code reviews and ensure deliverable quality
Coordinate with business teams on tasks and use cases
Write comprehensive technical documentation for delivered projects
Monitor production use cases and continuously improve performance and reliability


Profile & Qualifications

Technical Skills:


Strong experience in Data Engineering, with a proven ability to design and implement scalable data solutions
Extensive experience with large and complex datasets in Python, using libraries such as pandas
Solid foundation in software engineering best practices: clean code, version control, automated testing, workflow automation
Experience with data pipeline orchestration tools and/or Dataiku is a strong advantage
Experience in the finance industry and its products is a strong advantage


Soft Skills


Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving skills
Proactive, hands-on, and eager to learn new tools and technologies
Excellent communication skills and ability to translate technical solutions into business value
Comfortable working in a collaborative, agile, fast-paced environment
Fluent in English


Apply by submitting a CV in English.

About Euronext

Join Euronext – shape capital markets for future generations.

Euronext is the leading pan-European market infrastructure, connecting European economies to global capital markets to drive innovation and sustainable growth. Operating in 18 countries across Europe, the US, and Asia, it has regulated exchanges in Belgium, France, Ireland, Italy, the Netherlands, Norway, and Portugal.

The Group offers a wide range of products, including equities, FX, ETFs, bonds, derivatives, commodities, and advanced data services, and provides clearing and custody/settlement services. Euronext also offers technology, corporate, and data services to third parties.

Euronext Values

Unity


We respect and value the people we work with
We are unified through a common purpose
We embrace diversity and strive for inclusion


Integrity


We value transparency, communicate honestly and share information openly
We act with integrity in everything we do
We don’t hide our mistakes, and we learn from them


Agility


We act with a sense of urgency and decisiveness
We are adaptable, responsive and embrace change
We take smart risks


Energy


We are positively driven to make a difference and challenge the status quo
We focus on and encourage personal leadership
We motivate each other with our ambition


Accountability


We deliver maximum value to our customers and stakeholders
We take ownership and are accountable for the outcome
We reward and celebrate performance


We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, gender, age, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, or any other legally protected factor. We value the unique talents of all our people, who come from diverse backgrounds with different personal experiences and points of view and we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect.

Additional Information

This job description is only describing the main activities within a certain role and is not exhaustive. It does not prevent to add more tasks, projects.

Title: GCP & Python Data Engineer (Hibrido, Porto)
Description:
Descrição da Empresa

A Shore é uma empresa portuguesa de tecnologia que liga talento a inovação desde 2007. Especializada na gestão de talento tecnológico, deu em 2026 um novo salto, expandindo a sua oferta para áreas de Software Engineering, Data & AI e IT Core Services, posicionando-se como um parceiro end-to-end na transformação digital. Trabalhamos com alguns dos principais players de setores como banca, telecomunicações, saúde e setor público, desenvolvendo soluções escaláveis, orientadas a dados e com impacto real no negócio. Na Shore, combinamos know-how técnico, cultura de proximidade e foco nas pessoas para construir tecnologia com propósito e equipas preparadas para o futuro.

Descrição da Função

Join our team as a GCP & Python Data Engineer and help design and build scalable, cloud-native data solutions on Google Cloud Platform. You'll develop and optimise data pipelines and modern data platforms using Python and GCP technologies, while contributing to AI model operationalisation, automation, and robust testing practices to ensure reliable production-ready solutions. If you're passionate about cloud data engineering, Python development, and building scalable platforms that support advanced analytics and AI initiatives, we'd love to hear from you. Key Responsibilities: - Design and maintain end-to-end ELT/ETL pipelines for high-volume, multi-source data. - Build data models, warehouses and lakes on GCP using BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow, Dataproc and Pub/Sub. - Develop Python transformations, orchestration, automation and data-quality checks. - Integrate and operationalise AI prediction models within cloud data pipelines. - Plan and execute UAT and QA activities, data validation and release-readiness controls. - Implement monitoring, logging, alerting, governance, security, privacy and compliance controls. - Maintain technical documentation, data dictionaries, runbooks and test plans. Minimum requirements: - Strong Python development, including design patterns, testing, packaging and performance considerations. - Hands-on GCP experience with BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow / Beam, Dataproc, Pub/Sub and Cloud Composer / Airflow. - SQL, data modelling, schema design and production data-pipeline experience. - Understanding of AI prediction models, data preparation, feature stores and MLOps principles. - Git, orchestration and CI/CD for data pipelines. - French at C1 minimum, written and spoken, for active stakeholder engagement. - Professional English and strong communication, documentation and autonomous delivery skills.

Localização


Porto, Portugal

You'll develop and optimise data pipelines and modern data platforms using Python and GCP technologies, while contributing to AI model operationalisation, automation, and robust testing practices to ensure reliable production-ready solutions. If you're passionate about cloud data engineering, Python development, and building scalable platforms that support advanced analytics and AI initiatives, we'd love to hear from you. Key Responsibilities: - Design and maintain end-to-end ELT/ETL pipelines for high-volume, multi-source data. - Build data models, warehouses and lakes on GCP using BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow, Dataproc and Pub/Sub. - Develop Python transformations, orchestration, automation and data-quality checks. - Integrate and operationalise AI prediction models within cloud data pipelines. - Plan and execute UAT and QA activities, data validation and release-readiness controls. - Implement monitoring, logging, alerting, governance, security, privacy and compliance controls. - Maintain technical documentation, data dictionaries, runbooks and test plans. Minimum requirements: - Strong Python development, including design patterns, testing, packaging and performance considerations. - Hands-on GCP experience with BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow / Beam, Dataproc, Pub/Sub and Cloud Composer / Airflow. - SQL, data modelling, schema design and production data-pipeline experience. - Understanding of AI prediction models, data preparation, feature stores and MLOps principles. - Git, orchestration and CI/CD for data pipelines. - French at C1 minimum, written and spoken, for active stakeholder engagement. - Professional English and strong communication, documentation and autonomous delivery skills.

Localização

  • Porto, Portugal

Title: AI Expert
Description:
As an AI Expert, you will play a key role in shaping the future of digital parcel logistics, developing intelligent image-based recognition systems and control algorithms that drive industrial automation across Körber’s global supply chain solutions.

Your role in our team



In this role you will take end-to-end ownership of AI-based detection applications, covering everything from system architecture and data preparation to deployment in production environments
Design, implement, and optimize real-time software components for high-performance machine vision applications
Ensure the robustness and reliability of AI systems through thorough testing and validation, meeting industrial-grade quality and safety standards
Collaborate closely with multidisciplinary teams, including system architecture, mechatronics, software, and controls
Drive the continuous improvement of internal AI frameworks while integrating state-of-the-art open-source solutions and academic research
Contribute to the evolution of intelligent control algorithms used in automated logistics and parcel handling systems
Support the scalability and maintainability of AI solutions across global customer projects



Your profile



You hold a university degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, Electrical Engineering, or a related field
Ideally you have a PhD (completed or ongoing) or 5+ years of professional experience in Deep Learning and Machine Vision
Strong expertise in image segmentation, object detection, and image classification
Proficiency with Python and C++, including experience in scalable training on local and/or cloud infrastructures
Knowledge in Reinforcement Learning, Robotics, Generative AI, or Explainable AI (considered a plus)
You have a strong passion for applying AI to real-world industrial and logistics challenges
strong communication skills, working in an international, cross-functional environment
Structured, results-oriented, and proactive approach to work, combined with a hands-on mindset
You are fluent in English



Your Benefits



Exciting tasks in a fast-growing, future-oriented industry player
Competitive salary package, with extra-days off, including your birthday, flexible working hours and remote work option.
Health insurance for the whole family
Well-Being and Employee Assistance Program
Active protocols with external partners and other caring perks (ex: fresh fruit, bread and yogurt)
A pleasant and collaborative working atmosphere



Apply now and join our team!

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Us

Planet is a leading technology company transforming payments by putting customer experience first. We offer integrated solutions that include payment processing, VAT refunds, dynamic currency conversion, and management services for merchants in the Retail and Hospitality sectors worldwide.

In recent years, we have experienced significant growth, expanding our services and global presence.

With strong private equity investors, Advent International and Eurazeo, we have the financial capital and expertise to grow our capabilities and reach through acquisitions.

Our mission is to create a world of connected commerce where payments are simple, secure, and seamless, enabling our partners to deliver exceptional experiences to their customers.

Role Overview

As an Associate Analytics Engineer II, you will take increased ownership in building and maintaining analytics‑ready data models that support reporting, dashboards, and business decision‑making. This role builds on foundational analytics engineering skills and focuses on independent delivery, data quality, and consistent application of best practices using dbt, SQL, and Snowflake.

You will work closely with Senior Analytics Engineers, Data Analysts, Data Engineers, and business stakeholders to transform raw data into clean, tested, and well‑documented datasets. Compared to the AAE role, this position requires less day‑to‑day guidance and greater accountability for the quality and reliability of delivered models.

The AAE II is expected to be a reliable individual contributor, capable of owning well‑scoped analytics transformations end‑to‑end while continuing to grow toward senior‑level responsibilities.

What You Will Do

Design & Develop Analytics Models


Independently develop Bronze → Silver dbt models following established standards.
Contribute to Gold‑layer models and analytics outputs under guidance.
Apply dimensional modeling concepts (facts, dimensions) to support analytics and BI use cases.


Data Transformation & dbt Development


Use dbt to build, test, and document analytics models.
Refactor existing models to improve clarity, performance, or consistency.
Apply reusable patterns and macros where appropriate.


Data Quality & Testing


Implement appropriate dbt tests (e.g. not null, uniqueness, accepted values) for all owned models.
Validate data outputs against business logic and expected behaviour.
Identify potential data quality issues early and work with peers to resolve them.


Performance & Reliability


Be mindful of model performance and warehouse costs.
Apply basic optimisation techniques (e.g. filtering, incremental models) with guidance.
Monitor model runs and respond to failures or anomalies.


Collaboration & Stakeholder Support


Work closely with analysts to understand reporting and data requirements.
Support analytics use cases by ensuring data is well‑structured and clearly defined.
Communicate progress, risks, and blockers proactively.


Documentation & Knowledge Sharing


Own documentation for delivered models and columns using dbt.
Write clear, business‑friendly definitions aligned with shared terminology.
Contribute to shared team documentation, standards, or playbooks.


Continuous Improvement


Suggest improvements to existing models, tests, or workflows.
Learn and apply analytics engineering best practices.
Begin supporting junior engineers through knowledge sharing or informal guidance.


Who You Are


3–6 years of experience in analytics engineering, analytics, or a closely related role
Strong SQL skills and experience working with analytics datasets
Practical experience using dbt for transformations, tests, and documentation
Experience working with a modern data warehouse (e.g. Snowflake)
Familiarity with BI and analytics consumption patterns


Nice to Have


Exposure to semantic layers or shared metrics
Experience supporting Power BI or similar BI tools
Familiarity with Git‑based workflows and CI checks
Understanding of incremental modeling and performance tuning basics


What Success Looks Like


Independently delivers consistent, high‑quality Silver‑layer models
Applies testing and documentation without prompting
Requires limited review cycles for standard work
Demonstrates growing confidence in modeling decisions
Is viewed as a dependable contributor by analysts and peers


Why Planet

Planet is an equal opportunity employer where diversity is valued, and all employment is decided based on qualifications, merit, and business need.

Come and grow your career in the most exciting, fast paced technology market, with a business that delivers feel-good connected commerce. We would love to hear from you – Apply now.

At Planet, we embrace a hybrid work model, with three days a week in the office.

Reasonable accommodations may be made in order to allow for an individual to perform the essential functions of this role successfully.

Title: Investigador (m/f/d)
Description:
A Tua Missão

Se gostas da área de investigação, este desafio é para ti. Trata-se de uma posição dedicada a desenvolver investigação no âmbito do LA/P/0079/2020- Laboratório Associado em Energia, Transportes e Aeroespacial, financiado pela Fundação para a Ciência e Tecnologia (FCT), com impacto na área da Inteligência Artificial Científica, da otimização e da modelação computacional em engenharia, visando acelerar a maturação tecnológica (TRL), a validação e a transferência de resultados científicos.

Principais Responsabilidades


Aplicação de metodologias avançadas de Machine Learning, Deep Learning, modelos reduzidos e modelos orientados/informados por física a sistemas complexos de engenharia
Desenvolvimento e utilização de metodologias avançadas de IA científica, incluindo modelos substitutos, modelos generativos, modelos probabilísticos e técnicas de aprendizagem orientadas por dados e por física
Desenvolvimento de metodologias de otimização baseada em simulação, incluindo otimização Bayesiana, otimização sem derivadas, otimização multiobjetivo, calibração de modelos, identificação de parâmetros
Implementação de modelos de simulação, previsão, modelos computacionais acelerados por IA e gémeos digitais
Apoio à validação e maturação tecnológica (TRL) de resultados do projeto
Colaboração em equipas multidisciplinares e em múltiplas linhas de trabalho em paralelo
Produção de publicações científicas, software científico, ferramentas computacionais, relatórios técnicos e contributos para transferência de conhecimento


O Que Está Assegurado Para Ti


Participação em projetos desafiantes e inovadores, com elevado impacto e com geração de valor para a Indústria
Oportunidade de desenvolvimento pessoal e profissional, num ambiente estimulante, informal e de incentivo e valorização de novas ideias
Contrato de trabalho com condições salariais ajustadas à experiência, qualificações e responsabilidade da função
Horário de trabalho flexível e uma cultura que valoriza a conciliação entre vida profissional e pessoal
Seguro de saúde e acesso a uma rede de parcerias e protocolos, orientados para o bem-estar individual
22 dias de férias, acrescidos de 5 dias extra por ano, incluindo o dia de aniversário


Requisitos mínimos


Doutoramento em Engenharia
Experiência em Machine Learning, Deep Learning, modelos reduzidos ou modelos orientados por física
Conhecimento em simulação numérica, modelação computacional, otimização ou gémeos digitais
Experiência em otimização baseada em simulação, incluindo otimização Bayesiana, otimização sem derivadas, calibração de modelos ou identificação de parâmetros
Domínio de Python e frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc.)
Conhecimentos de HPC, computação científica, cloud computing, MLOps ou desenvolvimento de software científico
Produção científica e tecnológica relevante, incluindo publicações, software científico, ferramentas computacionais, relatórios técnicos, patentes ou contributos para transferência de conhecimento
Experiência na aplicação de metodologias de IA, otimização ou modelação computacional a casos-estudo das áreas de energia, transportes, aeroespacial, defesa, indústria resiliente, materiais ou estruturas
Pensamento crítico e orientação para resultados
Excelentes competências de comunicação escrita e oral
Capacidade de atuação em ambientes multidisciplinares e de trabalho em equipa
Autonomia e rigor científico
Domínio da língua inglesa, oral e escrita. O conhecimento da língua portuguesa será valorizado
